verses.text: “You shall not oppress an alien, for you know the heart of an alien, since you were aliens in the land of Egypt.
verses.explainer: You shall not oppress a sojourner. You know the heart of a sojourner, for you were sojourners in Egypt. The second time this command appears -- repetition signals importance.

verses.text: “For six years you shall sow your land, and shall gather in its increase,
verses.explainer: For six years you shall sow your land and gather its yield.

verses.text: but the seventh year you shall let it rest and lie fallow, that the poor of your people may eat; and what they leave the animal of the field shall eat. In the same way, you shall deal with your vineyard and with your olive grove.
verses.explainer: But the seventh year you shall let it rest and lie fallow, that the poor of your people may eat; and what they leave the beasts of the field may eat. The Sabbath principle applies to land. Every seventh year, the poor eat freely from what grows on its own.

verses.text: “Six days you shall do your work, and on the seventh day you shall rest, that your ox and your donkey may have rest, and the son of your servant, and the alien may be refreshed.
verses.explainer: Six days you shall do your work, but on the seventh day you shall rest, that your ox and donkey may have rest, and the son of your servant woman and the alien may be refreshed. The Sabbath protects animals, servants, and immigrants.

verses.text: “Be careful to do all things that I have said to you; and don’t invoke the name of other gods or even let them be heard out of your mouth.
verses.explainer: Pay attention to all that I have said to you. Make no mention of the names of other gods; let them not be heard on your lips.

verses.text: “You shall observe a feast to me three times a year.
verses.explainer: Three times in the year you shall keep a feast to me. The three pilgrimage festivals structure Israel's year around worship.

verses.text: You shall observe the feast of unleavened bread. Seven days you shall eat unleavened bread, as I commanded you, at the time appointed in the month Abib (for in it you came out of Egypt), and no one shall appear before me empty.
verses.explainer: You shall keep the Feast of Unleavened Bread. Seven days you shall eat unleavened bread, as I commanded you, at the time appointed in the month of Abib, for in it you came out of Egypt. No one shall appear before me empty-handed.

culturalNotes.title: Cultural Context
culturalNotes.note: {'term': 'Three pilgrimage festivals', 'explanation': 'The three festivals -- Unleavened Bread (Passover), Harvest (Pentecost/Shavuot), and Ingathering (Tabernacles/Sukkot) -- required every male Israelite to appear before God at the central sanctuary. They combined agricultural thanksgiving with historical memory: Passover (liberation), Pentecost (law-giving), and Tabernacles (wilderness journey). All three become significant in the New Testament.'}

verses.text: And the feast of harvest, the first fruits of your labors, which you sow in the field; and the feast of ingathering, at the end of the year, when you gather in your labors out of the field.
verses.explainer: You shall keep the Feast of Harvest, of the firstfruits of your labor, of what you sow in the field. You shall keep the Feast of Ingathering at the end of the year, when you gather in your labor from the field.

verses.text: Three times in the year all your males shall appear before the Lord Yahweh.
verses.explainer: Three times in the year shall all your males appear before the Lord GOD.

verses.text: “You shall not offer the blood of my sacrifice with leavened bread. The fat of my feast shall not remain all night until the morning.
verses.explainer: You shall not offer the blood of my sacrifice with anything leavened, or let the fat of my feast remain until morning.

verses.text:
The first of the first fruits of your ground you shall bring into the house of Yahweh your God.
“You shall not boil a young goat in its mother’s milk.
verses.explainer: The best of the firstfruits of your ground you shall bring into the house of the LORD your God. You shall not boil a young goat in its mother's milk. A prohibition likely targeting a Canaanite fertility ritual.

verses.text: “Behold, I send an angel before you, to keep you by the way, and to bring you into the place which I have prepared.
verses.explainer: Behold, I send an angel before you to guard you on the way and to bring you to the place I have prepared. God provides supernatural guidance and protection for the journey ahead.

verses.text: Pay attention to him, and listen to his voice. Don’t provoke him, for he will not pardon your disobedience, for my name is in him.
verses.explainer: Pay careful attention to him and obey his voice. Do not rebel against him, for he will not pardon your transgression, for my name is in him. The angel carries God's authority and name.

verses.text: But if you indeed listen to his voice, and do all that I speak, then I will be an enemy to your enemies, and an adversary to your adversaries.
verses.explainer: But if you carefully obey his voice and do all that I say, then I will be an enemy to your enemies.

verses.text: For my angel shall go before you, and bring you in to the Amorite, the Hittite, the Perizzite, the Canaanite, the Hivite, and the Jebusite; and I will cut them off.
verses.explainer: My angel will go before you and bring you to the Amorites, Hittites, Perizzites, Canaanites, Hivites, and Jebusites. I will blot them out.

verses.text: You shall not bow down to their gods, nor serve them, nor follow their practices, but you shall utterly overthrow them and demolish their pillars.
verses.explainer: You shall not bow down to their gods or serve them or do as they do. You shall utterly overthrow them and break their pillars in pieces.

verses.text: You shall serve Yahweh your God, and he will bless your bread and your water, and I will take sickness away from among you.
verses.explainer: You shall serve the LORD your God, and he will bless your bread and your water. I will take sickness away from your midst.

verses.text: No one will miscarry or be barren in your land. I will fulfill the number of your days.
verses.explainer: None shall miscarry or be barren in your land. I will fulfill the number of your days.

verses.text: I will send my terror before you, and will confuse all the people to whom you come, and I will make all your enemies turn their backs to you.
verses.explainer: I will send my terror before you and will throw into confusion all the people against whom you shall come. I will make all your enemies turn their backs to you.

verses.text: I will send the hornet before you, which will drive out the Hivite, the Canaanite, and the Hittite, from before you.
verses.explainer: I will send hornets before you, which shall drive out the Hivites, the Canaanites, and the Hittites from before you.

verses.text: I will not drive them out from before you in one year, lest the land become desolate, and the animals of the field multiply against you.
verses.explainer: I will not drive them out from before you in one year, lest the land become desolate and the wild beasts multiply against you. Conquest will be gradual -- God has practical reasons for patience.

verses.text: Little by little I will drive them out from before you, until you have increased and inherit the land.
verses.explainer: Little by little I will drive them out, until you have increased and possess the land. Growth takes time.

verses.text: I will set your border from the Red Sea even to the sea of the Philistines, and from the wilderness to the River; for I will deliver the inhabitants of the land into your hand, and you shall drive them out before you.
verses.explainer: I will set your border from the Red Sea to the Sea of the Philistines, and from the wilderness to the Euphrates. The maximum extent of the promised territory.

verses.text: You shall make no covenant with them, nor with their gods.
verses.explainer: You shall make no covenant with them and their gods.

verses.text: They shall not dwell in your land, lest they make you sin against me, for if you serve their gods, it will surely be a snare to you.”
verses.explainer: They shall not dwell in your land, lest they make you sin against me. If you serve their gods, it will surely be a snare to you. The warning is prophetic -- this is exactly what will happen.

explainer.summary: The covenant is ratified in one of the most extraordinary scenes in the Bible. Moses reads the Book of the Covenant to the people, and they say: 'All the LORD has spoken we will do, and we will be obedient.' Moses sprinkles sacrificial blood on the altar and on the people -- blood of the covenant. Then Moses, Aaron, Nadab, Abihu, and seventy elders go up the mountain and see God. Under his feet is something like a pavement of sapphire, as clear as the sky itself. They eat and drink in God's presence. Then God calls Moses up higher for forty days and forty nights to receive the stone tablets.
themes: Eating and drinking in God's presence
explainer.contextInBook: This is the climactic covenant ceremony. The law has been given (chs. 20-23), and now it is formally ratified with blood, oaths, and a vision of God. What follows (chs. 25-31) will be the instructions Moses receives during his forty days on the mountain.

wordStudies.translatedAs: The blood sprinkled on both the altar (representin
wordStudies.meaning: The blood sprinkled on both the altar (representing God) and the people seals the covenant between them. It binds both parties in a life-and-death commitment. Jesus uses this exact phrase at the Last Supper: 'This is my blood of the covenant, which is poured out for many.'

verses.text: He said to Moses, “Come up to Yahweh, you, and Aaron, Nadab, and Abihu, and seventy of the elders of Israel; and worship from a distance.
verses.explainer: God says to Moses: Come up to the LORD, you and Aaron, Nadab and Abihu, and seventy of the elders of Israel, and worship from afar.

verses.text: Moses alone shall come near to Yahweh, but they shall not come near. The people shall not go up with him.”
verses.explainer: Moses alone shall come near to the LORD. The others shall not come near, and the people shall not come up with him. Degrees of access to God's presence.

verses.text: Moses came and told the people all Yahweh’s words, and all the ordinances; and all the people answered with one voice, and said, “All the words which Yahweh has spoken will we do.”
verses.explainer: Moses comes and tells the people all the words of the LORD and all the rules. All the people answer with one voice: 'All the words the LORD has spoken we will do.' Unanimous consent.

verses.text: Moses wrote all Yahweh’s words, and rose up early in the morning, and built an altar under the mountain, and twelve pillars for the twelve tribes of Israel.
verses.explainer: Moses writes down all the words of the LORD. He rises early and builds an altar at the foot of the mountain with twelve pillars according to the twelve tribes. The covenant is documented and symbolized.

verses.text: He sent young men of the children of Israel, who offered burnt offerings and sacrificed peace offerings of cattle to Yahweh.
verses.explainer: He sends young men of the Israelites to offer burnt offerings and sacrifice peace offerings of oxen to the LORD.

verses.text: Moses took half of the blood and put it in basins, and half of the blood he sprinkled on the altar.
verses.explainer: Moses takes half the blood and puts it in basins. Half the blood he throws against the altar.

verses.text: He took the book of the covenant and read it in the hearing of the people, and they said, “All that Yahweh has spoken will we do, and be obedient.”
verses.explainer: He takes the Book of the Covenant and reads it in the hearing of the people. They say: 'All that the LORD has spoken we will do, and we will be obedient.' The second oath -- now with the full text read aloud.

verses.text: Moses took the blood, and sprinkled it on the people, and said, “Look, this is the blood of the covenant, which Yahweh has made with you concerning all these words.”
verses.explainer: Moses takes the blood and throws it on the people, saying: 'Behold the blood of the covenant that the LORD has made with you in accordance with all these words.' The covenant is sealed in blood.

verses.text: Then Moses, Aaron, Nadab, Abihu, and seventy of the elders of Israel went up.
verses.explainer: Then Moses, Aaron, Nadab, Abihu, and seventy elders of Israel go up.

verses.text:
They saw the God of Israel. Under his feet was like a paved work of sapphire
 stone, like the skies for clearness.
verses.explainer: They see the God of Israel. Under his feet is something like a pavement of sapphire stone, like the very heaven for clearness. The most remarkable sentence in the chapter -- they see God and live.

verses.text: He didn’t lay his hand on the nobles of the children of Israel. They saw God, and ate and drank.
verses.explainer: He did not lay his hand on the chief men of the Israelites. They beheld God, and they ate and drank. They share a covenant meal in God's visible presence. The closest thing to Eden since the Fall.

verses.text: Yahweh said to Moses, “Come up to me on the mountain, and stay here, and I will give you the stone tablets with the law and the commands that I have written, that you may teach them.”
verses.explainer: The LORD says to Moses: Come up to me on the mountain and wait there. I will give you the tablets of stone, with the law and the commandment, which I have written for their instruction. God writes the law himself.

verses.text: Moses rose up with Joshua, his servant, and Moses went up onto God’s Mountain.
verses.explainer: Moses rises with his assistant Joshua, and Moses goes up the mountain of God.

verses.text: He said to the elders, “Wait here for us, until we come again to you. Behold, Aaron and Hur are with you. Whoever is involved in a dispute can go to them.”
verses.explainer: He tells the elders: Wait here until we return. Aaron and Hur are with you. Whoever has a dispute, let him go to them. This arrangement will be tested catastrophically in chapter 32.

verses.text: Moses went up on the mountain, and the cloud covered the mountain.
verses.explainer: Moses goes up the mountain, and the cloud covers it.

verses.text: Yahweh’s glory settled on Mount Sinai, and the cloud covered it six days. The seventh day he called to Moses out of the middle of the cloud.
verses.explainer: The glory of the LORD dwells on Mount Sinai, and the cloud covers it six days. On the seventh day he calls to Moses out of the cloud. Even the mountaintop encounter follows the seven-day pattern.

verses.text: The appearance of Yahweh’s glory was like devouring fire on the top of the mountain in the eyes of the children of Israel.
verses.explainer: The appearance of the glory of the LORD is like a devouring fire on the top of the mountain in the sight of the Israelites.

verses.text: Moses entered into the middle of the cloud, and went up on the mountain; and Moses was on the mountain forty days and forty nights.
verses.explainer: Moses enters the cloud and goes up the mountain. Moses is on the mountain forty days and forty nights. The number forty appears again -- the period of preparation and testing.

thenVsNow.then: Forty days without food at the summit of a mountain in the direct presence of God. Moses is sustained not by bread but by divine encounter. This sets the pattern for Jesus' forty-day fast in the wilderness.
thenVsNow.now: The number forty in the Bible consistently marks periods of transformation: Noah's flood, Moses on Sinai, Israel in the wilderness, Jesus in the desert. It suggests that deep change requires sustained time in God's presence.

explainer.summary: God gives Moses detailed instructions for building the Tabernacle -- a portable sanctuary where God will dwell among his people. The plan begins with the most sacred object: the Ark of the Covenant, a gold-covered box containing the tablets of the law, topped with two cherubim and the 'mercy seat' where God's presence dwells. Then come the table for the bread of the Presence and the golden lampstand (menorah). The materials come from the people's voluntary offerings.
themes: The Ark as the throne of God's presence
Sacred architecture as theology in physical form
The mercy seat as the place of atonement
explainer.contextInBook: Chapters 25-31 are God's instructions to Moses on the mountain. The Tabernacle is God's answer to the question implied by the Exodus: now that Israel is free, how will God live among them? The Tabernacle is a portable Eden -- God's dwelling place restored.

wordStudies.translatedAs: From the verb shakan, 'to dwell
wordStudies.meaning: From the verb shakan, 'to dwell.' The Tabernacle is literally the 'dwelling place.' The same root gives us Shekinah -- the visible manifestation of God's presence. God does not merely visit his people; he moves in.

wordStudies.translatedAs: From the verb kaphar, 'to cover' or 'to atone
wordStudies.meaning: From the verb kaphar, 'to cover' or 'to atone.' The mercy seat is the lid of the Ark where God's presence dwells and where the high priest sprinkles blood on the Day of Atonement. It is simultaneously God's throne and the place of forgiveness.

verses.text: Yahweh spoke to Moses, saying,
verses.explainer: The LORD speaks to Moses.

verses.text: “Speak to the children of Israel, that they take an offering for me. From everyone whose heart makes him willing you shall take my offering.
verses.explainer: Speak to the Israelites, that they take for me a contribution. From every man whose heart moves him you shall receive the contribution. The Tabernacle is funded by voluntary giving, not taxation.

verses.text: This is the offering which you shall take from them: gold, silver, brass,
verses.explainer: This is the contribution you shall receive: gold, silver, and bronze.

verses.text: blue, purple, scarlet, fine linen, goats’ hair,
verses.explainer: Blue, purple, and scarlet yarns and fine twined linen, goats' hair.

verses.text: rams’ skins dyed red, sea cow hides, acacia wood,
verses.explainer: Tanned rams' skins, goatskins, acacia wood.

verses.text: oil for the light, spices for the anointing oil and for the sweet incense,
verses.explainer: Oil for the lamps, spices for the anointing oil and for the fragrant incense.

verses.text: onyx stones, and stones to be set for the ephod and for the breastplate.
verses.explainer: Onyx stones and stones for setting, for the ephod and the breastpiece.

verses.text: Let them make me a sanctuary, that I may dwell among them.
verses.explainer: Let them make me a sanctuary, that I may dwell in their midst. This single sentence is the purpose statement for everything that follows. God wants to live with his people.

verses.text: According to all that I show you, the pattern of the tabernacle, and the pattern of all of its furniture, even so you shall make it.
verses.explainer: Exactly as I show you the pattern of the tabernacle and all its furnishings, so you shall make it. The design comes from heaven. Moses is given a blueprint, not creative freedom.

verses.text: “They shall make an ark of acacia wood. Its length shall be two and a half cubits, its width a cubit and a half, and a cubit and a half its height.
verses.explainer: They shall make an ark of acacia wood. Two and a half cubits long, a cubit and a half wide, a cubit and a half high. The most sacred object in Israel begins with a wooden box.

verses.text: You shall overlay it with pure gold. You shall overlay it inside and outside, and you shall make a gold molding around it.
verses.explainer: You shall overlay it with pure gold, inside and out, and make a molding of gold around it.

verses.text: You shall cast four rings of gold for it, and put them in its four feet. Two rings shall be on the one side of it, and two rings on the other side of it.
verses.explainer: Cast four rings of gold for it, two on each side.

verses.text: You shall make poles of acacia wood, and overlay them with gold.
verses.explainer: Make poles of acacia wood and overlay them with gold.

verses.text: You shall put the poles into the rings on the sides of the ark to carry the ark.
verses.explainer: Put the poles into the rings on the sides of the ark to carry it.

verses.text: The poles shall be in the rings of the ark. They shall not be taken from it.
verses.explainer: The poles shall remain in the rings of the ark; they shall not be removed from it. The Ark is always ready to move. God's presence is portable.

verses.text: You shall put the testimony which I shall give you into the ark.
verses.explainer: In the ark you shall put the testimony that I shall give you. The stone tablets of the law are the ark's contents -- God's words at the center of his presence.

verses.text: You shall make a mercy seat of pure gold. Two and a half cubits shall be its length, and a cubit and a half its width.
verses.explainer: You shall make a mercy seat of pure gold, two and a half cubits long and a cubit and a half wide. The mercy seat (kapporet) is the lid of the Ark and the most sacred surface in Israel.

verses.text: You shall make two cherubim of hammered gold. You shall make them at the two ends of the mercy seat.
verses.explainer: Make two cherubim of gold. Make them of hammered work at the two ends of the mercy seat.

verses.text: Make one cherub at the one end, and one cherub at the other end. You shall make the cherubim on its two ends of one piece with the mercy seat.
verses.explainer: Make one cherub at one end and one at the other. Of one piece with the mercy seat you shall make the cherubim at its two ends.

verses.text: The cherubim shall spread out their wings upward, covering the mercy seat with their wings, with their faces toward one another. The faces of the cherubim shall be toward the mercy seat.
verses.explainer: The cherubim shall spread out their wings above, overshadowing the mercy seat with their wings, their faces toward each other, toward the mercy seat.

verses.text: You shall put the mercy seat on top of the ark, and in the ark you shall put the testimony that I will give you.
verses.explainer: Put the mercy seat on top of the ark. In the ark put the testimony I shall give you.

verses.text: There I will meet with you, and I will tell you from above the mercy seat, from between the two cherubim which are on the ark of the testimony, all that I command you for the children of Israel.
verses.explainer: There I will meet with you. From above the mercy seat, from between the two cherubim on the ark of the testimony, I will speak with you about all I will give you in commandment for the Israelites. The mercy seat is God's speaking place -- his throne of grace.

thenVsNow.then: The space between the cherubim was considered the earthly throne of God -- the place where heaven and earth intersected. God spoke from there to Moses as king speaks from a throne.
thenVsNow.now: Hebrews 4:16 calls believers to 'approach the throne of grace with confidence.' The mercy seat -- the meeting place between God and humanity -- is now accessible through Christ. The architecture of the Tabernacle becomes the theology of access to God.

verses.text: “You shall make a table of acacia wood. Two cubits shall be its length, and a cubit its width, and one and a half cubits its height.
verses.explainer: You shall make a table of acacia wood, two cubits long, a cubit wide, and a cubit and a half high.

verses.text: You shall overlay it with pure gold, and make a gold molding around it.
verses.explainer: Overlay it with pure gold and make a molding of gold around it.

verses.text: You shall make a rim of a hand width around it. You shall make a golden molding on its rim around it.
verses.explainer: Make a rim a handbreadth wide around it and a molding of gold around the rim.

verses.text: You shall make four rings of gold for it, and put the rings in the four corners that are on its four feet.
verses.explainer: Make four rings of gold for it and fasten them at the four corners of its four legs.

verses.text: the rings shall be close to the rim, for places for the poles to carry the table.
verses.explainer: The rings shall be close to the rim as holders for the poles to carry the table.

verses.text: You shall make the poles of acacia wood, and overlay them with gold, that the table may be carried with them.
verses.explainer: Make the poles of acacia wood and overlay them with gold. The table shall be carried by them.

verses.text: You shall make its dishes, its spoons, its ladles, and its bowls to pour out offerings with. You shall make them of pure gold.
verses.explainer: Make its plates, dishes, pitchers, and bowls for pouring drink offerings. Make them of pure gold.

verses.text: You shall set bread of the presence on the table before me always.
verses.explainer: You shall set the bread of the Presence on the table before me always. Twelve loaves representing the twelve tribes, always in God's presence -- a perpetual reminder that God sustains and sees his people.

culturalNotes.title: Cultural Context
culturalNotes.note: {'term': 'Bread of the Presence', 'explanation': "Hebrew: lechem hapanim, literally 'bread of the face' -- bread set before God's face. Twelve loaves were placed on the table every Sabbath, and the old loaves were eaten by the priests. The bread symbolized God's perpetual provision and Israel's perpetual communion with him. It anticipated Jesus' words: 'I am the bread of life.'"}

verses.text: “You shall make a lamp stand of pure gold. Of hammered work shall the lamp stand be made, even its base, its shaft, its cups, its buds, and its flowers, shall be of one piece with it.
verses.explainer: You shall make a lampstand of pure gold. The lampstand shall be made of hammered work: its base, shaft, cups, calyxes, and flowers shall be of one piece.

verses.text: There shall be six branches going out of its sides: three branches of the lamp stand out of its one side, and three branches of the lamp stand out of its other side;
verses.explainer: Six branches shall go out of its sides -- three from one side and three from the other.

verses.text: three cups made like almond blossoms in one branch, a bud and a flower; and three cups made like almond blossoms in the other branch, a bud and a flower, so for the six branches going out of the lamp stand;
verses.explainer: Three cups made like almond blossoms on one branch, with calyx and flower, and three on the next branch. So for all six branches.

verses.text: and in the lamp stand four cups made like almond blossoms, its buds and its flowers;
verses.explainer: On the lampstand itself there shall be four cups shaped like almond blossoms, with calyxes and flowers.

verses.text: and a bud under two branches of one piece with it, and a bud under two branches of one piece with it, and a bud under two branches of one piece with it, for the six branches going out of the lamp stand.
verses.explainer: A calyx of one piece with it under each pair of the six branches going out from the lampstand.

verses.text: Their buds and their branches shall be of one piece with it, all of it one beaten work of pure gold.
verses.explainer: Their calyxes and branches shall be of one piece with it, the whole of it a single piece of hammered work of pure gold.

verses.text: You shall make its lamps seven, and they shall light its lamps to give light to the space in front of it.
verses.explainer: You shall make seven lamps for it, and the lamps shall be set up so as to give light on the space in front of it. Seven is the number of completion; the light fills the holy place.

verses.text: Its snuffers and its snuff dishes shall be of pure gold.
verses.explainer: Its tongs and trays shall be of pure gold.

verses.text: It shall be made of a talent of pure gold, with all these accessories.
verses.explainer: It shall be made, with all these utensils, out of a talent of pure gold. A talent is roughly 75 pounds of gold -- an extraordinary amount.

verses.text: See that you make them after their pattern, which has been shown to you on the mountain.
verses.explainer: See that you make them after the pattern which is being shown you on the mountain. The heavenly pattern must be followed exactly. The earthly Tabernacle reflects a heavenly reality.

explainer.summary: Detailed instructions for the Tabernacle's fabric walls and wooden framework. The structure uses ten curtains of fine linen embroidered with cherubim, joined by gold clasps. Over these go curtains of goats' hair, then rams' skins dyed red, and finally goatskins. The framework consists of acacia wood frames standing upright in silver bases, held together by crossbars. A veil of blue, purple, and scarlet yarn embroidered with cherubim separates the Holy Place from the Most Holy Place, where the Ark dwells.
themes: Layers of covering: beauty hidden within protection
The veil as the boundary between God and humanity
The Most Holy Place as God's inner dwelling
explainer.contextInBook: The Tabernacle's structure creates a graduated approach to God: the outer court (accessible to all Israelites), the Holy Place (priests only), and the Most Holy Place (high priest once a year). The architecture teaches theology: God is both near and holy.

verses.text: “Moreover you shall make the tabernacle with ten curtains; of fine twined linen, and blue, and purple, and scarlet, with cherubim. The work of the skillful workman you shall make them.
verses.explainer: You shall make the tabernacle with ten curtains of fine twined linen and blue, purple, and scarlet yarns. You shall make them with cherubim skillfully worked into them. The innermost curtains are the most beautiful -- the glory is on the inside.

verses.text: The length of each curtain shall be twenty-eight cubits, and the width of each curtain four cubits: all the curtains shall have one measure.
verses.explainer: The length of each curtain shall be twenty-eight cubits, and the breadth four cubits. All curtains shall be the same size.

verses.text: Five curtains shall be coupled together to one another; and the other five curtains shall be coupled to one another.
verses.explainer: Five curtains shall be coupled to one another, and the other five curtains coupled to one another.

verses.text: You shall make loops of blue on the edge of the one curtain from the edge in the coupling; and likewise you shall make in the edge of the curtain that is outermost in the second coupling.
verses.explainer: You shall make loops of blue on the edge of the outermost curtain in the first set and on the edge of the outermost curtain in the second set.

verses.text: You shall make fifty loops in the one curtain, and you shall make fifty loops in the edge of the curtain that is in the second coupling. The loops shall be opposite one another.
verses.explainer: Fifty loops on one curtain, and fifty loops on the edge of the curtain in the second set, the loops being opposite one another.

verses.text: You shall make fifty clasps of gold, and couple the curtains to one another with the clasps: and the tabernacle shall be a unit.
verses.explainer: Make fifty clasps of gold, and couple the curtains one to another with the clasps so that the tabernacle may be a single whole.

verses.text: “You shall make curtains of goats’ hair for a covering over the tabernacle. You shall make them eleven curtains.
verses.explainer: You shall make curtains of goats' hair for a tent over the tabernacle. Eleven curtains shall you make.

verses.text: The length of each curtain shall be thirty cubits, and the width of each curtain four cubits: the eleven curtains shall have one measure.
verses.explainer: The length of each curtain shall be thirty cubits, and the breadth four cubits. The eleven curtains shall be the same size.

verses.text: You shall couple five curtains by themselves, and six curtains by themselves, and shall double over the sixth curtain in the forefront of the tent.
verses.explainer: Join five curtains by themselves, and six curtains by themselves, and double over the sixth curtain in the front of the tent.

verses.text: You shall make fifty loops on the edge of the one curtain that is outermost in the coupling, and fifty loops on the edge of the curtain which is outermost in the second coupling.
verses.explainer: Make fifty loops on the edge of the outermost curtain of the one set, and fifty loops on the edge of the curtain of the other set.

verses.text: You shall make fifty clasps of brass, and put the clasps into the loops, and couple the tent together, that it may be one.
verses.explainer: Make fifty clasps of bronze and put them into the loops and couple the tent together as one whole.

verses.text: The overhanging part that remains of the curtains of the tent, the half curtain that remains, shall hang over the back of the tabernacle.
verses.explainer: The part that remains of the tent curtains, the half curtain that remains, shall hang over the back of the tabernacle.

verses.text: The cubit on the one side, and the cubit on the other side, of that which remains in the length of the curtains of the tent, shall hang over the sides of the tabernacle on this side and on that side, to cover it.
verses.explainer: The extra cubit on each side in the length of the tent curtains shall hang over the sides to cover it.

verses.text: You shall make a covering for the tent of rams’ skins dyed red, and a covering of sea cow hides above.
verses.explainer: Make a covering for the tent of tanned rams' skins and a covering of goatskins on top. Four layers: linen with cherubim, goats' hair, red rams' skins, goatskins. From the outside, the Tabernacle looks rough and plain. All the beauty is within.

thenVsNow.then: The layered coverings provided weather protection while creating a striking contrast: the exterior was plain leather, but the interior was dazzling with gold, embroidered linen, and lamplight reflecting off polished surfaces.
thenVsNow.now: The contrast between the Tabernacle's plain exterior and glorious interior has been used as a metaphor for spiritual life: what matters most is what is inside, not the outward appearance.

verses.text: “You shall make the boards for the tabernacle of acacia wood, standing up.
verses.explainer: You shall make upright frames for the tabernacle of acacia wood.

verses.text: Ten cubits shall be the length of a board, and one and a half cubits the width of each board.
verses.explainer: Ten cubits shall be the length of a frame, and a cubit and a half the breadth.

verses.text: There shall be two tenons in each board, joined to one another: thus you shall make for all the boards of the tabernacle.
verses.explainer: Each frame shall have two tenons for fitting together. Do this for all the frames.

verses.text: You shall make the boards for the tabernacle, twenty boards for the south side southward.
verses.explainer: Make the frames: twenty frames for the south side.

verses.text: You shall make forty sockets of silver under the twenty boards; two sockets under one board for its two tenons, and two sockets under another board for its two tenons.
verses.explainer: Make forty bases of silver under the twenty frames -- two bases under each frame for its two tenons.

verses.text: For the second side of the tabernacle, on the north side, twenty boards,
verses.explainer: For the second side, the north side, twenty frames.

verses.text: and their forty sockets of silver; two sockets under one board, and two sockets under another board.
verses.explainer: And their forty bases of silver -- two bases under each frame.

verses.text: For the far part of the tabernacle westward you shall make six boards.
verses.explainer: For the rear of the tabernacle westward, make six frames.

verses.text: You shall make two boards for the corners of the tabernacle in the far part.
verses.explainer: Make two frames for the corners of the rear.

verses.text: They shall be double beneath, and in the same way they shall be whole to its top to one ring: thus shall it be for them both; they shall be for the two corners.
verses.explainer: They shall be separate beneath but joined at the top at the first ring. So it shall be for both of them; they shall form the two corners.

verses.text: There shall be eight boards, and their sockets of silver, sixteen sockets; two sockets under one board, and two sockets under another board.
verses.explainer: There shall be eight frames with their silver bases: sixteen bases, two under each frame.

verses.text: “You shall make bars of acacia wood: five for the boards of the one side of the tabernacle,
verses.explainer: Make bars of acacia wood: five for the frames of one side of the tabernacle.

verses.text: and five bars for the boards of the other side of the tabernacle, and five bars for the boards of the side of the tabernacle, for the far part westward.
verses.explainer: And five bars for the frames of the other side, and five bars for the rear westward.

verses.text: The middle bar in the middle of the boards shall pass through from end to end.
verses.explainer: The middle bar, halfway up the frames, shall run from end to end.

verses.text: You shall overlay the boards with gold, and make their rings of gold for places for the bars: and you shall overlay the bars with gold.
verses.explainer: Overlay the frames with gold and make rings of gold as holders for the bars. Overlay the bars with gold.

verses.text: You shall set up the tabernacle according to the way that it was shown to you on the mountain.
verses.explainer: Erect the tabernacle according to the plan shown you on the mountain.

verses.text: “You shall make a veil of blue, and purple, and scarlet, and fine twined linen, with cherubim. The work of the skillful workman shall it be made.
verses.explainer: You shall make a veil of blue, purple, and scarlet yarns and fine twined linen. It shall be made with cherubim skillfully worked into it. The veil separates the Holy Place from the Most Holy Place -- the barrier between humanity and God's direct presence.

verses.text: You shall hang it on four pillars of acacia overlaid with gold; their hooks shall be of gold, on four sockets of silver.
verses.explainer: Hang it on four pillars of acacia wood overlaid with gold, with hooks of gold, on four bases of silver.

verses.text: You shall hang up the veil under the clasps, and shall bring the ark of the testimony in there within the veil: and the veil shall separate the holy place from the most holy for you.
verses.explainer: Hang the veil from the clasps and bring the ark of the testimony in there behind the veil. The veil shall separate for you the Holy Place from the Most Holy. The Ark -- God's throne -- is hidden behind the veil.

verses.text: You shall put the mercy seat on the ark of the testimony in the most holy place.
verses.explainer: Put the mercy seat on the ark of the testimony in the Most Holy Place.

verses.text: You shall set the table outside the veil, and the lamp stand over against the table on the side of the tabernacle toward the south: and you shall put the table on the north side.
verses.explainer: Set the table outside the veil on the north side, and the lampstand on the south side opposite the table.

verses.text: “You shall make a screen for the door of the Tent, of blue, and purple, and scarlet, and fine twined linen, the work of the embroiderer.
verses.explainer: Make a screen for the entrance of the tent of blue, purple, and scarlet yarns and fine twined linen, embroidered with needlework.

verses.text: You shall make for the screen five pillars of acacia, and overlay them with gold: their hooks shall be of gold: and you shall cast five sockets of brass for them.
verses.explainer: Make five pillars of acacia for the screen and overlay them with gold. Their hooks shall be of gold, and you shall cast five bases of bronze for them.

explainer.summary: Instructions for the bronze altar of burnt offering -- the first thing encountered upon entering the Tabernacle courtyard. Then the courtyard itself, enclosed by linen curtains on bronze posts. Finally, instructions for pure olive oil to keep the lampstand burning continuously. The bronze altar is where sacrifices are offered; the courtyard is where the people gather; the oil keeps the light burning in the holy place.
themes: The altar as the center of worship
The courtyard as sacred space accessible to all Israel
Bronze for the outer areas, gold for the inner
explainer.contextInBook: The description moves outward from the most sacred objects (Ark, table, lampstand) to the structure (curtains, frames, veil) to the courtyard. The architecture creates zones of increasing holiness as one moves inward.

verses.text: “You shall make the altar of acacia wood, five cubits long, and five cubits wide; the altar shall be square: and its height shall be three cubits.
verses.explainer: You shall make the altar of acacia wood, five cubits long and five cubits wide -- the altar shall be square -- and three cubits high. The bronze altar is the largest piece of furniture in the Tabernacle complex.

verses.text: You shall make its horns on its four corners; its horns shall be of one piece with it; and you shall overlay it with brass.
verses.explainer: Make horns on its four corners. The horns shall be of one piece with it. Overlay it with bronze.

verses.text: You shall make its pots to take away its ashes, its shovels, its basins, its meat hooks, and its fire pans: all its vessels you shall make of brass.
verses.explainer: Make its pots for ashes, shovels, basins, forks, and fire pans. All its utensils you shall make of bronze.

verses.text: You shall make a grating for it of network of brass: and on the net you shall make four bronze rings in its four corners.
verses.explainer: Make for it a grating, a network of bronze. On the net make four bronze rings at its four corners.

verses.text: You shall put it under the ledge around the altar beneath, that the net may reach halfway up the altar.
verses.explainer: Set it under the ledge of the altar so that the net extends halfway down the altar.

verses.text: You shall make poles for the altar, poles of acacia wood, and overlay them with brass.
verses.explainer: Make poles for the altar, poles of acacia wood, and overlay them with bronze.

verses.text: Its poles shall be put into the rings, and the poles shall be on the two sides of the altar, when carrying it.
verses.explainer: The poles shall be put through the rings so that the poles are on the two sides of the altar when it is carried. Portable, like everything in the Tabernacle.

verses.text: You shall make it with hollow planks. They shall make it as it has been shown you on the mountain.
verses.explainer: You shall make it hollow, with boards, as it has been shown you on the mountain, so shall it be made.

verses.text: “You shall make the court of the tabernacle: for the south side southward there shall be hangings for the court of fine twined linen one hundred cubits long for one side:
verses.explainer: You shall make the court of the tabernacle. For the south side, hangings of fine twined linen a hundred cubits long for one side.

verses.text: and its pillars shall be twenty, and their sockets twenty, of brass; the hooks of the pillars and their fillets shall be of silver.
verses.explainer: Its twenty pillars and twenty bases shall be of bronze, with hooks and bands of silver on the pillars.

verses.text: Likewise for the north side in length there shall be hangings one hundred cubits long, and its pillars twenty, and their sockets twenty, of brass; the hooks of the pillars, and their fillets, of silver.
verses.explainer: For the north side likewise, hangings a hundred cubits long, with twenty pillars and twenty bases of bronze, and hooks and bands of silver.

verses.text: For the width of the court on the west side shall be hangings of fifty cubits; their pillars ten, and their sockets ten.
verses.explainer: For the width of the court on the west side, hangings of fifty cubits, with ten pillars and ten bases.

verses.text: The width of the court on the east side eastward shall be fifty cubits.
verses.explainer: The width of the court on the front, the east side, shall be fifty cubits.

verses.text: The hangings for the one side of the gate shall be fifteen cubits; their pillars three, and their sockets three.
verses.explainer: Hangings of fifteen cubits shall be for one side, with three pillars and three bases.

verses.text: For the other side shall be hangings of fifteen cubits; their pillars three, and their sockets three.
verses.explainer: And fifteen cubits of hangings for the other side, with three pillars and three bases.

verses.text: For the gate of the court shall be a screen of twenty cubits, of blue, and purple, and scarlet, and fine twined linen, the work of the embroiderer; their pillars four, and their sockets four.
verses.explainer: For the gate of the court there shall be a screen twenty cubits long, of blue, purple, and scarlet yarns and fine twined linen, embroidered with needlework. It shall have four pillars and four bases.

verses.text: All the pillars of the court around shall be filleted with silver; their hooks of silver, and their sockets of brass.
verses.explainer: All the pillars around the court shall be banded with silver. Their hooks shall be of silver, and their bases of bronze.

verses.text: The length of the court shall be one hundred cubits, and the width fifty throughout, and the height five cubits, of fine twined linen, and their sockets of brass.
verses.explainer: The length of the court shall be a hundred cubits, the width fifty, and the height five cubits, with hangings of fine twined linen and bases of bronze. The courtyard is roughly 150 feet by 75 feet.

verses.text: All the instruments of the tabernacle in all its service, and all its pins, and all the pins of the court, shall be of brass.
verses.explainer: All the utensils of the tabernacle for every use, and all its pegs and all the pegs of the court, shall be of bronze.

verses.text: “You shall command the children of Israel, that they bring to you pure olive oil beaten for the light, to cause a lamp to burn continually.
verses.explainer: Command the Israelites to bring you pure beaten olive oil for the light, that a lamp may regularly be set up to burn.

culturalNotes.title: Cultural Context
culturalNotes.note: {'term': 'Pure beaten olive oil', 'explanation': 'The finest olive oil was made by crushing olives in a mortar rather than pressing them, producing the clearest, most refined oil with the steadiest flame. This oil was expensive and labor-intensive to produce, reflecting the principle that what is offered to God should be the best available.'}

verses.text: In the Tent of Meeting, outside the veil which is before the testimony, Aaron and his sons shall keep it in order from evening to morning before Yahweh: it shall be a statute forever throughout their generations on the behalf of the children of Israel.
verses.explainer: In the tent of meeting, outside the veil before the testimony, Aaron and his sons shall tend it from evening to morning before the LORD. It shall be a statute forever throughout their generations from the Israelites. The light never goes out in God's house.

explainer.summary: God commands Moses to consecrate Aaron and his sons as priests and to make sacred garments for them 'for glory and for beauty.' The high priest's vestments include the ephod (a decorated vest), the breastpiece with twelve precious stones representing the twelve tribes, the Urim and Thummim (instruments for discerning God's will), the robe with golden bells, the turban with a gold plate engraved 'Holy to the LORD.' The priest carries Israel on his shoulders (ephod stones) and over his heart (breastpiece stones) when he enters God's presence.
themes: The priesthood as mediating God's presence
The high priest as a picture of Christ
explainer.contextInBook: The priesthood is the human institution that makes the Tabernacle functional. Without priests, the sacrificial system cannot operate. The elaborate garments communicate the gravity and beauty of representing God to the people and the people to God.

wordStudies.translatedAs: Literally 'lights and perfections
wordStudies.meaning: Literally 'lights and perfections.' These were objects kept in the breastpiece of the high priest, used to determine God's will in matters of national importance. Their exact nature is unknown -- possibly marked stones or gems used to receive yes/no answers from God. They represent the principle that Israel's leaders should seek divine guidance, not act on human wisdom alone.

verses.text: “Bring Aaron your brother, and his sons with him, near to you from among the children of Israel, that he may minister to me in the priest’s office, even Aaron, Nadab and Abihu, Eleazar and Ithamar, Aaron’s sons.
verses.explainer: Bring near to you Aaron your brother, and his sons with him, from among the Israelites, to serve me as priests -- Aaron, Nadab, Abihu, Eleazar, and Ithamar.

verses.text: You shall make holy garments for Aaron your brother, for glory and for beauty.
verses.explainer: Make sacred garments for Aaron your brother, for glory and for beauty. The priestly garments have a dual purpose: they glorify God and they are beautiful. Worship engages aesthetics.

verses.text: You shall speak to all who are wise-hearted, whom I have filled with the spirit of wisdom, that they make Aaron’s garments to sanctify him, that he may minister to me in the priest’s office.
verses.explainer: Speak to all the skillful, whom I have filled with a spirit of skill, that they make Aaron's garments to consecrate him. God fills craftsmen with his Spirit for artistic work -- the first mention of the Spirit empowering art.

verses.text: These are the garments which they shall make: a breastplate, and an ephod, and a robe, and a coat of checker work, a turban, and a sash: and they shall make holy garments for Aaron your brother, and his sons, that he may minister to me in the priest’s office.
verses.explainer: These are the garments they shall make: a breastpiece, an ephod, a robe, a coat of checker work, a turban, and a sash.

verses.text: They shall take the gold, and the blue, and the purple, and the scarlet, and the fine linen.
verses.explainer: They shall receive gold, blue, purple, scarlet yarns, and fine twined linen.

verses.text: “They shall make the ephod of gold, of blue, and purple, scarlet, and fine twined linen, the work of the skillful workman.
verses.explainer: They shall make the ephod of gold, blue, purple, scarlet yarns, and fine twined linen, skillfully worked.

verses.text: It shall have two shoulder straps joined to the two ends of it, that it may be joined together.
verses.explainer: It shall have two shoulder pieces attached to its two edges, so that it may be joined together.

verses.text: The skillfully woven band, which is on it, that is on him, shall be like its work and of the same piece; of gold, of blue, and purple, and scarlet, and fine twined linen.
verses.explainer: The skillfully woven band on it shall be of the same workmanship and of one piece with it.

verses.text: You shall take two onyx stones, and engrave on them the names of the children of Israel:
verses.explainer: Take two onyx stones and engrave on them the names of the sons of Israel.

verses.text: six of their names on the one stone, and the names of the six that remain on the other stone, in the order of their birth.
verses.explainer: Six names on one stone, six on the other, in the order of their birth.

verses.text: With the work of an engraver in stone, like the engravings of a signet, you shall engrave the two stones, according to the names of the children of Israel: you shall make them to be enclosed in settings of gold.
verses.explainer: As a jeweler engraves signets, so you shall engrave the two stones with the names of the sons of Israel. Set them in gold filigree settings.

verses.text: You shall put the two stones on the shoulder straps of the ephod, to be stones of memorial for the children of Israel: and Aaron shall bear their names before Yahweh on his two shoulders for a memorial.
verses.explainer: Set the two stones on the shoulder pieces of the ephod as stones of remembrance for the sons of Israel. Aaron shall bear their names before the LORD on his two shoulders for remembrance. The priest carries the nation on his shoulders into God's presence.

thenVsNow.then: The high priest literally wore Israel's names into the Most Holy Place. He did not enter God's presence as an individual but as a representative carrying the entire nation.
thenVsNow.now: Christians see in the high priest a picture of Christ, who carries his people's names on his shoulders and his heart into God's presence. Hebrews 7:25 says Christ 'always lives to make intercession' -- he permanently carries us before God.

verses.text: You shall make settings of gold,
verses.explainer: Make settings of gold filigree.

verses.text: and two chains of pure gold; you shall make them like cords of braided work: and you shall put the braided chains on the settings.
verses.explainer: And two chains of pure gold, twisted like cords, and attach the corded chains to the settings.

verses.text: “You shall make a breastplate of judgment, the work of the skillful workman; like the work of the ephod you shall make it; of gold, of blue, and purple, and scarlet, and fine twined linen, you shall make it.
verses.explainer: Make a breastpiece of judgment, in skilled work, like the work of the ephod.

verses.text: It shall be square and folded double; a span shall be its length of it, and a span its width.
verses.explainer: It shall be square and doubled, a span its length and a span its breadth.

verses.text: You shall set in it settings of stones, four rows of stones: a row of ruby, topaz, and beryl shall be the first row;
verses.explainer: Set in it four rows of stones. A row of sardius, topaz, and carbuncle shall be the first row.

verses.text: and the second row a turquoise, a sapphire, and an emerald;
verses.explainer: The second row: an emerald, a sapphire, and a diamond.

verses.text: and the third row a jacinth, an agate, and an amethyst;
verses.explainer: The third row: a jacinth, an agate, and an amethyst.

verses.text: and the fourth row a chrysolite, an onyx, and a jasper: they shall be enclosed in gold in their settings.
verses.explainer: The fourth row: a beryl, an onyx, and a jasper. They shall be set in gold filigree. Twelve stones for twelve tribes -- each tribe represented by a precious gem.

verses.text: The stones shall be according to the names of the children of Israel, twelve, according to their names; like the engravings of a signet, everyone according to his name, they shall be for the twelve tribes.
verses.explainer: There shall be twelve stones, one for each name of the sons of Israel, each engraved like a signet with its name, for the twelve tribes.

verses.text: You shall make on the breastplate chains like cords, of braided work of pure gold.
verses.explainer: Make for the breastpiece twisted chains like cords, of pure gold.

verses.text: You shall make on the breastplate two rings of gold, and shall put the two rings on the two ends of the breastplate.
verses.explainer: Make two rings of gold for the breastpiece and put them on the two edges.

verses.text: You shall put the two braided chains of gold in the two rings at the ends of the breastplate.
verses.explainer: Put the two cords of gold in the two rings at the edges of the breastpiece.

verses.text: The other two ends of the two braided chains you shall put on the two settings, and put them on the shoulder straps of the ephod in its forepart.
verses.explainer: Attach the other ends of the two cords to the two settings of filigree, and so attach them to the shoulder pieces of the ephod at the front.

verses.text: You shall make two rings of gold, and you shall put them on the two ends of the breastplate, on its edge, which is toward the side of the ephod inward.
verses.explainer: Make two rings of gold and put them at the two ends of the breastpiece, on the inside edge next to the ephod.

verses.text: You shall make two rings of gold, and shall put them on the two shoulder straps of the ephod underneath, in its forepart, close by its coupling, above the skillfully woven band of the ephod.
verses.explainer: Make two rings of gold and attach them to the lower part of the two shoulder pieces of the ephod, at the front, close to the seam above the skillfully woven waistband.

verses.text: They shall bind the breastplate by its rings to the rings of the ephod with a lace of blue, that it may be on the skillfully woven band of the ephod, and that the breastplate may not swing out from the ephod.
verses.explainer: They shall bind the breastpiece by its rings to the rings of the ephod with a cord of blue, so that it may lie on the waistband and not come loose from the ephod.

verses.text: Aaron shall bear the names of the children of Israel in the breastplate of judgment on his heart, when he goes in to the holy place, for a memorial before Yahweh continually.
verses.explainer: Aaron shall bear the names of the sons of Israel on the breastpiece of judgment over his heart, when he goes into the Holy Place, to bring them to regular remembrance before the LORD. He carries them on his shoulders and over his heart -- strength and love.

verses.text: You shall put in the breastplate of judgment the Urim and the Thummim; and they shall be on Aaron’s heart, when he goes in before Yahweh: and Aaron shall bear the judgment of the children of Israel on his heart before Yahweh continually.
verses.explainer: In the breastpiece of judgment put the Urim and Thummim, and they shall be over Aaron's heart when he goes in before the LORD. Aaron shall bear the judgment of the Israelites over his heart before the LORD regularly.

verses.text: “You shall make the robe of the ephod all of blue.
verses.explainer: Make the robe of the ephod all of blue.

verses.text: It shall have a hole for the head in the middle of it. It shall have a binding of woven work around its hole, as it were the hole of a coat of mail, that it not be torn.
verses.explainer: It shall have an opening for the head in the middle of it, with a woven binding around the opening like a collar, so that it may not tear.

verses.text: On its hem you shall make pomegranates of blue, and of purple, and of scarlet, around its hem; and bells of gold between and around them:
verses.explainer: On its hem you shall make pomegranates of blue, purple, and scarlet yarns, around its hem, with bells of gold between them.

verses.text: a golden bell and a pomegranate, a golden bell and a pomegranate, around the hem of the robe.
verses.explainer: A golden bell and a pomegranate, a golden bell and a pomegranate, around the hem of the robe. Alternating bells and pomegranates -- sound and fruit.

verses.text: It shall be on Aaron to minister: and its sound shall be heard when he goes in to the holy place before Yahweh, and when he comes out, that he not die.
verses.explainer: It shall be on Aaron when he ministers. Its sound shall be heard when he goes into the Holy Place before the LORD and when he comes out, so that he does not die. The bells announce the priest's movements -- his presence before God is audible.

culturalNotes.title: Cultural Context
culturalNotes.note: {'term': 'Bells on the priestly robe', 'explanation': "The bells served both practical and theological purposes. Practically, the sound told the people outside that the high priest was still alive and moving in God's presence. Theologically, the priest could not enter God's presence silently or secretly -- his approach was announced. The sound of the bells was the sound of mediation in progress."}

verses.text: “You shall make a plate of pure gold, and engrave on it, like the engravings of a signet, ‘HOLY TO YAHWEH.’
verses.explainer: Make a plate of pure gold and engrave on it, like the engraving of a signet: 'Holy to the LORD.'

verses.text: You shall put it on a lace of blue, and it shall be on the sash; on the front of the sash it shall be.
verses.explainer: Fasten it on the turban by a cord of blue. It shall be on the front of the turban.

verses.text: It shall be on Aaron’s forehead, and Aaron shall bear the iniquity of the holy things, which the children of Israel shall make holy in all their holy gifts; and it shall be always on his forehead, that they may be accepted before Yahweh.
verses.explainer: It shall be on Aaron's forehead, and Aaron shall bear any guilt from the holy things the Israelites consecrate. It shall always be on his forehead, that they may be accepted before the LORD. The priest bears the people's guilt so their offerings are accepted.

verses.text: You shall weave the coat in checker work of fine linen, and you shall make a turban of fine linen, and you shall make a sash, the work of the embroiderer.
verses.explainer: Weave the coat in checker work of fine linen. Make a turban of fine linen and make a sash embroidered with needlework.

verses.text: “You shall make coats for Aaron’s sons, and you shall make sashes for them and you shall make headbands for them, for glory and for beauty.
verses.explainer: For Aaron's sons make coats, sashes, and caps, for glory and beauty.

verses.text: You shall put them on Aaron your brother, and on his sons with him, and shall anoint them, and consecrate them, and sanctify them, that they may minister to me in the priest’s office.
verses.explainer: Put them on Aaron your brother and on his sons with him. Anoint them, ordain them, and consecrate them, that they may serve me as priests.

verses.text: You shall make them linen breeches to cover the flesh of their nakedness; from the waist even to the thighs they shall reach:
verses.explainer: Make linen undergarments for them to cover their bare flesh, from the hips to the thighs.

verses.text: They shall be on Aaron, and on his sons, when they go in to the Tent of Meeting, or when they come near to the altar to minister in the holy place; that they don’t bear iniquity, and die: it shall be a statute forever to him and to his offspring after him.
verses.explainer: Aaron and his sons shall wear them when they go into the tent of meeting or approach the altar to minister in the Holy Place, lest they bear guilt and die. It shall be a statute forever for him and his offspring. Proper worship requires proper preparation.

explainer.summary: The elaborate seven-day ordination ceremony for the priests. Moses is to wash Aaron and his sons, dress them in the sacred garments, anoint them with oil, and offer a bull and two rams as sacrifices. Blood from the ram of ordination is placed on Aaron's right ear, thumb, and big toe -- consecrating his hearing, his work, and his walk. The chapter concludes with God's promise: 'I will dwell among the Israelites and will be their God. They shall know that I am the LORD their God, who brought them out of Egypt that I might dwell among them.'
themes: God's ultimate purpose: dwelling with his people
explainer.contextInBook: The ordination ceremony transforms ordinary men into priests who can approach God on behalf of the people. The elaborate ritual emphasizes that mediating between a holy God and sinful people is not casual work.

verses.text: “This is the thing that you shall do to them to make them holy, to minister to me in the priest’s office: take one young bull and two rams without defect,
verses.explainer: This is what you shall do to consecrate them to serve me as priests. Take one young bull and two rams without blemish.

verses.text: unleavened bread, unleavened cakes mixed with oil, and unleavened wafers anointed with oil: you shall make them of fine wheat flour.
verses.explainer: And unleavened bread, unleavened cakes mixed with oil, and unleavened wafers smeared with oil. Make them of fine wheat flour.

verses.text: You shall put them into one basket, and bring them in the basket, with the bull and the two rams.
verses.explainer: Put them in a basket and bring them in the basket, along with the bull and the two rams.

verses.text: You shall bring Aaron and his sons to the door of the Tent of Meeting, and shall wash them with water.
verses.explainer: Bring Aaron and his sons to the entrance of the tent of meeting and wash them with water. Cleansing precedes consecration.

verses.text: You shall take the garments, and put on Aaron the coat, the robe of the ephod, the ephod, and the breastplate, and clothe him with the skillfully woven band of the ephod;
verses.explainer: Take the garments and put on Aaron the coat, the robe of the ephod, the ephod, and the breastpiece, and gird him with the skillfully woven band of the ephod.

verses.text: and you shall set the turban on his head, and put the holy crown on the turban.
verses.explainer: Set the turban on his head and put the holy crown on the turban.

verses.text: Then you shall take the anointing oil, and pour it on his head, and anoint him.
verses.explainer: Take the anointing oil and pour it on his head and anoint him. Anointing with oil symbolizes the Spirit's empowerment for service.

verses.text: You shall bring his sons, and put coats on them.
verses.explainer: Bring his sons and put coats on them.

verses.text: You shall clothe them with belts, Aaron and his sons, and bind headbands on them: and they shall have the priesthood by a perpetual statute: and you shall consecrate Aaron and his sons.
verses.explainer: Gird Aaron and his sons with sashes. Bind caps on them. The priesthood shall be theirs by a statute forever. Thus you shall ordain Aaron and his sons.

verses.text: “You shall bring the bull before the Tent of Meeting: and Aaron and his sons shall lay their hands on the head of the bull.
verses.explainer: Bring the bull before the tent of meeting. Aaron and his sons shall lay their hands on the head of the bull.

verses.text: You shall kill the bull before Yahweh, at the door of the Tent of Meeting.
verses.explainer: Kill the bull before the LORD at the entrance of the tent of meeting.

verses.text: You shall take of the blood of the bull, and put it on the horns of the altar with your finger; and you shall pour out all the blood at the base of the altar.
verses.explainer: Take some of the blood of the bull and put it on the horns of the altar with your finger, and pour out all the rest of the blood at the base of the altar.

verses.text: You shall take all the fat that covers the innards, the cover of the liver, the two kidneys, and the fat that is on them, and burn them on the altar.
verses.explainer: Take all the fat that covers the entrails, the long lobe of the liver, and the two kidneys with the fat on them, and burn them on the altar.

verses.text: But the meat of the bull, and its skin, and its dung, you shall burn with fire outside of the camp: it is a sin offering.
verses.explainer: But the flesh of the bull, its skin, and its dung you shall burn outside the camp. It is a sin offering.

verses.text: “You shall also take the one ram; and Aaron and his sons shall lay their hands on the head of the ram.
verses.explainer: Take one of the rams. Aaron and his sons shall lay their hands on the head of the ram.

verses.text: You shall kill the ram, and you shall take its blood, and sprinkle it around on the altar.
verses.explainer: Kill the ram, take its blood, and throw it against the sides of the altar.

verses.text: You shall cut the ram into its pieces, and wash its innards, and its legs, and put them with its pieces, and with its head.
verses.explainer: Cut the ram into pieces, wash its entrails and legs, and put them with its pieces and its head.

verses.text: You shall burn the whole ram on the altar: it is a burnt offering to Yahweh; it is a pleasant aroma, an offering made by fire to Yahweh.
verses.explainer: Burn the whole ram on the altar. It is a burnt offering to the LORD, a pleasing aroma, a food offering to the LORD.

verses.text: “You shall take the other ram; and Aaron and his sons shall lay their hands on the head of the ram.
verses.explainer: Take the other ram. Aaron and his sons shall lay their hands on its head.

verses.text: Then you shall kill the ram, and take some of its blood, and put it on the tip of the right ear of Aaron, and on the tip of the right ear of his sons, and on the thumb of their right hand, and on the big toe of their right foot, and sprinkle the blood around on the altar.
verses.explainer: Kill the ram. Take some of its blood and put it on the tip of the right ear of Aaron and on the tips of the right ears of his sons, and on the thumbs of their right hands and on the great toes of their right feet. Throw the rest of the blood against the sides of the altar. Blood on the ear, hand, and foot: everything the priest hears, does, and everywhere he goes is consecrated.

thenVsNow.then: The blood on the ear, thumb, and toe consecrated the whole person for service. The priest's hearing (receiving God's word), his hands (doing God's work), and his feet (walking God's path) were all claimed by God.
thenVsNow.now: The principle extends beyond priesthood: total consecration means every part of life -- what we listen to, what we do with our hands, where we go -- belongs to God.

verses.text: You shall take of the blood that is on the altar, and of the anointing oil, and sprinkle it on Aaron, and on his garments, and on his sons, and on the garments of his sons with him: and he shall be made holy, and his garments, and his sons, and his sons’ garments with him.
verses.explainer: Take some of the blood on the altar and some of the anointing oil and sprinkle it on Aaron and his garments, and on his sons and their garments. He and his garments shall be holy, and his sons and their garments.

verses.text: Also you shall take some of the ram’s fat, the fat tail, the fat that covers the innards, the cover of the liver, the two kidneys, the fat that is on them, and the right thigh (for it is a ram of consecration),
verses.explainer: Take from the ram the fat, the fat tail, the fat on the entrails, the long lobe of the liver, the two kidneys with their fat, and the right thigh, for it is a ram of ordination.

verses.text: and one loaf of bread, one cake of oiled bread, and one wafer out of the basket of unleavened bread that is before Yahweh.
verses.explainer: And one loaf of bread, one cake of bread made with oil, and one wafer out of the basket of unleavened bread before the LORD.

verses.text: You shall put all of this in Aaron’s hands, and in his sons’ hands, and shall wave them for a wave offering before Yahweh.
verses.explainer: Put all these in the hands of Aaron and in the hands of his sons and wave them as a wave offering before the LORD.

verses.text: You shall take them from their hands, and burn them on the altar on the burnt offering, for a pleasant aroma before Yahweh: it is an offering made by fire to Yahweh.
verses.explainer: Then take them from their hands and burn them on the altar on top of the burnt offering, as a pleasing aroma before the LORD. It is a food offering to the LORD.

verses.text: “You shall take the breast of Aaron’s ram of consecration, and wave it for a wave offering before Yahweh: and it shall be your portion.
verses.explainer: Take the breast of the ram of Aaron's ordination and wave it as a wave offering before the LORD. It shall be your portion.

verses.text: You shall sanctify the breast of the wave offering, and the thigh of the wave offering, which is waved, and which is heaved up, of the ram of consecration, even of that which is for Aaron, and of that which is for his sons:
verses.explainer: Consecrate the breast of the wave offering and the thigh of the contribution that is waved and raised from the ram of ordination -- from what was Aaron's and his sons'.

verses.text: and it shall be for Aaron and his sons as their portion forever from the children of Israel; for it is a wave offering: and it shall be a wave offering from the children of Israel of the sacrifices of their peace offerings, even their wave offering to Yahweh.
verses.explainer: It shall be for Aaron and his sons as a perpetual due from the Israelites, for it is a contribution.

verses.text: “The holy garments of Aaron shall be for his sons after him, to be anointed in them, and to be consecrated in them.
verses.explainer: The sacred garments of Aaron shall be for his sons after him. They shall be anointed and ordained in them. The garments are passed down -- the priesthood is hereditary.

verses.text: Seven days shall the son who is priest in his place put them on, when he comes into the Tent of Meeting to minister in the holy place.
verses.explainer: The son who succeeds him as priest, who comes into the tent of meeting to minister in the Holy Place, shall wear them seven days.

verses.text: “You shall take the ram of consecration, and boil its meat in a holy place.
verses.explainer: Take the ram of ordination and boil its flesh in a holy place.

verses.text: Aaron and his sons shall eat the meat of the ram, and the bread that is in the basket, at the door of the Tent of Meeting.
verses.explainer: Aaron and his sons shall eat the flesh of the ram and the bread in the basket at the entrance of the tent of meeting.

verses.text: They shall eat those things with which atonement was made, to consecrate and sanctify them: but a stranger shall not eat of it, because they are holy.
verses.explainer: They shall eat those things with which atonement was made at their ordination and consecration. No outsider shall eat of them because they are holy.

verses.text: If anything of the meat of the consecration, or of the bread, remains to the morning, then you shall burn the remainder with fire: it shall not be eaten, because it is holy.
verses.explainer: If any of the flesh or bread remains until morning, burn the remainder. It shall not be eaten because it is holy.

verses.text: “You shall do so to Aaron, and to his sons, according to all that I have commanded you. You shall consecrate them seven days.
verses.explainer: Do to Aaron and his sons according to all I have commanded you. Through seven days you shall ordain them. Seven days for a complete transformation.

verses.text: Every day you shall offer the bull of sin offering for atonement: and you shall cleanse the altar, when you make atonement for it; and you shall anoint it, to sanctify it.
verses.explainer: Every day you shall offer a bull as a sin offering for atonement. Purify the altar when you make atonement for it, and anoint it to consecrate it.

verses.text: Seven days you shall make atonement for the altar, and sanctify it: and the altar shall be most holy; whatever touches the altar shall be holy.
verses.explainer: Seven days you shall make atonement for the altar and consecrate it. The altar shall be most holy. Whatever touches the altar shall become holy.

verses.text: “Now this is that which you shall offer on the altar: two lambs a year old day by day continually.
verses.explainer: This is what you shall offer on the altar: two lambs a year old, day by day regularly.

verses.text: The one lamb you shall offer in the morning; and the other lamb you shall offer at evening:
verses.explainer: One lamb you shall offer in the morning, and the other lamb you shall offer at twilight. The daily sacrifice frames each day between two acts of worship.

verses.text: and with the one lamb a tenth part of an ephah of fine flour mixed with the fourth part of a hin of beaten oil, and the fourth part of a hin of wine for a drink offering.
verses.explainer: With the first lamb, a tenth measure of fine flour mingled with a fourth of a hin of beaten oil, and a fourth of a hin of wine for a drink offering.

verses.text: The other lamb you shall offer at evening, and shall do to it according to the meal offering of the morning, and according to its drink offering, for a pleasant aroma, an offering made by fire to Yahweh.
verses.explainer: The other lamb you shall offer at twilight with a grain offering and drink offering like the morning, as a pleasing aroma, a food offering to the LORD.

verses.text: It shall be a continual burnt offering throughout your generations at the door of the Tent of Meeting before Yahweh, where I will meet with you, to speak there to you.
verses.explainer: It shall be a regular burnt offering throughout your generations at the entrance of the tent of meeting before the LORD, where I will meet with you, to speak to you there.

verses.text: There I will meet with the children of Israel; and the place shall be sanctified by my glory.
verses.explainer: There I will meet with the Israelites, and it shall be sanctified by my glory.

verses.text: I will sanctify the Tent of Meeting and the altar: Aaron also and his sons I will sanctify, to minister to me in the priest’s office.
verses.explainer: I will consecrate the tent of meeting and the altar. Aaron and his sons I will consecrate to serve me as priests.

verses.text: I will dwell among the children of Israel, and will be their God.
verses.explainer: I will dwell among the Israelites and will be their God. The purpose of the Tabernacle in a single sentence: divine presence among the people.

verses.text: They shall know that I am Yahweh their God, who brought them out of the land of Egypt, that I might dwell among them: I am Yahweh their God.
verses.explainer: They shall know that I am the LORD their God, who brought them out of Egypt that I might dwell among them. I am the LORD their God. The purpose of the Exodus is not political freedom but divine communion. God freed Israel so he could live with them.

explainer.summary: Additional Tabernacle furnishings and provisions. The golden altar of incense stands before the veil, filling the Holy Place with fragrant smoke -- a symbol of prayer rising to God. A census tax of half a shekel per person funds the Tabernacle (rich and poor pay the same). A bronze basin for priestly washing is placed between the altar and the tent. Sacred anointing oil and incense are formulated with specific ingredients and reserved exclusively for sacred use.
themes: Incense as prayer rising to God
Equality before God in the census offering
Sacred things reserved for sacred purposes
explainer.contextInBook: These additional instructions complete the Tabernacle plan. Each item serves a specific theological function: the incense altar represents prayer, the basin represents cleansing, the anointing oil represents consecration, and the census tax represents equal participation.

wordStudies.translatedAs: A specially compounded fragrant blend burned on th
wordStudies.meaning: A specially compounded fragrant blend burned on the golden altar. The rising smoke symbolizes prayer ascending to God. Psalm 141:2 says, 'Let my prayer be counted as incense before you.' In Revelation 5:8, the elders hold golden bowls of incense 'which are the prayers of the saints.'

verses.text: “You shall make an altar to burn incense on. You shall make it of acacia wood.
verses.explainer: You shall make an altar on which to burn incense. Make it of acacia wood.

verses.text: Its length shall be a cubit, and its width a cubit. It shall be square, and its height shall be two cubits. Its horns shall be of one piece with it.
verses.explainer: A cubit shall be its length and a cubit its breadth. It shall be square, and two cubits shall be its height. Its horns shall be of one piece with it.
