verses.text: He made four pillars of acacia for it, and overlaid them with gold. Their hooks were of gold. He cast four sockets of silver for them.
verses.explainer: He makes four pillars of acacia for it and overlays them with gold, with hooks of gold. He casts four bases of silver for them.

verses.text: He made a screen for the door of the tent, of blue, purple, scarlet, and fine twined linen, the work of an embroiderer;
verses.explainer: He makes a screen for the entrance of the tent, of blue, purple, and scarlet yarns and fine twined linen, embroidered with needlework.

verses.text: and the five pillars of it with their hooks. He overlaid their capitals and their fillets with gold, and their five sockets were of brass.
verses.explainer: He makes its five pillars with their hooks. He overlays their capitals and fillets with gold, but their five bases are of bronze.

explainer.summary: Bezalel constructs the most sacred furnishings: the Ark of the Covenant with its mercy seat and cherubim, the table for the bread of the Presence, the golden lampstand with seven lamps, and the altar of incense. He also makes the sacred anointing oil and the pure incense. Each item is built exactly according to God's specifications from chapters 25 and 30.
themes: Faithful execution of the divine design
The sacred objects as theology in material form
explainer.contextInBook: The construction chapters (35-39) mirror the instruction chapters (25-31) in reverse. What God described from the inside out (Ark first), Bezalel builds following the same order. The repetition emphasizes that obedience is not creative reinterpretation but faithful execution.

verses.text: Bezalel made the ark of acacia wood. Its length was two and a half cubits, and its width a cubit and a half, and a cubit and a half its height.
verses.explainer: Bezalel makes the ark of acacia wood. Two cubits and a half its length, a cubit and a half its breadth, and a cubit and a half its height. The most sacred object is constructed first.

verses.text: He overlaid it with pure gold inside and outside, and made a molding of gold for it around it.
verses.explainer: He overlays it with pure gold inside and outside and makes a molding of gold around it.

verses.text: He cast four rings of gold for it, in its four feet; even two rings on its one side, and two rings on its other side.
verses.explainer: He casts four rings of gold for it, two on each side.

verses.text: He made poles of acacia wood, and overlaid them with gold.
verses.explainer: He makes poles of acacia wood and overlays them with gold.

verses.text: He put the poles into the rings on the sides of the ark, to bear the ark.
verses.explainer: He puts the poles into the rings on the sides of the ark to carry it.

verses.text: He made a mercy seat of pure gold. Its length was two and a half cubits, and a cubit and a half its width.
verses.explainer: He makes a mercy seat of pure gold. Two cubits and a half its length, a cubit and a half its breadth.

verses.text: He made two cherubim of gold. He made them of beaten work, at the two ends of the mercy seat;
verses.explainer: He makes two cherubim of gold. He makes them of hammered work on the two ends of the mercy seat.

verses.text: one cherub at the one end, and one cherub at the other end. He made the cherubim of one piece with the mercy seat at its two ends.
verses.explainer: One cherub on one end and one on the other. Of one piece with the mercy seat he makes the cherubim on its two ends.

verses.text: The cherubim spread out their wings on high, covering the mercy seat with their wings, with their faces toward one another. The faces of the cherubim were toward the mercy seat.
verses.explainer: The cherubim spread out their wings above, overshadowing the mercy seat. Their faces are toward each other, toward the mercy seat. The throne of God is complete.

verses.text: He made the table of acacia wood. Its length was two cubits, and its width was a cubit, and its height was a cubit and a half.
verses.explainer: He makes the table of acacia wood. Two cubits its length, a cubit its breadth, and a cubit and a half its height.

verses.text: He overlaid it with pure gold, and made a gold molding around it.
verses.explainer: He overlays it with pure gold and makes a molding of gold around it.

verses.text: He made a border of a hand width around it, and made a golden molding on its border around it.
verses.explainer: He makes a rim a handbreadth wide around it and a molding of gold around the rim.

verses.text: He cast four rings of gold for it, and put the rings in the four corners that were on its four feet.
verses.explainer: He casts four rings of gold for it and fastens them at the four corners of its four legs.

verses.text: The rings were close by the border, the places for the poles to carry the table.
verses.explainer: Close to the rim the rings are, as holders for the poles to carry the table.

verses.text: He made the poles of acacia wood, and overlaid them with gold, to carry the table.
verses.explainer: He makes the poles of acacia wood to carry the table and overlays them with gold.

verses.text: He made the vessels which were on the table, its dishes, its spoons, its bowls, and its pitchers with which to pour out, of pure gold.
verses.explainer: He makes the vessels of pure gold that are to be on the table: its plates, dishes, bowls, and pitchers for pouring drink offerings.

verses.text: He made the lamp stand of pure gold. He made the lamp stand of beaten work. Its base, its shaft, its cups, its buds, and its flowers were of one piece with it.
verses.explainer: He makes the lampstand of pure gold, of hammered work. Its base, shaft, cups, calyxes, and flowers are of one piece with it. The seven-branched menorah -- one of the most recognized symbols in the world.

verses.text: There were six branches going out of its sides: three branches of the lamp stand out of its one side, and three branches of the lamp stand out of its other side:
verses.explainer: Six branches go out of its sides, three from one side and three from the other.

verses.text: three cups made like almond blossoms in one branch, a bud and a flower, and three cups made like almond blossoms in the other branch, a bud and a flower: so for the six branches going out of the lamp stand.
verses.explainer: Three cups shaped like almond blossoms on one branch, with calyx and flower, and three on the next. So for all six branches.

verses.text: In the lamp stand were four cups made like almond blossoms, its buds and its flowers;
verses.explainer: On the lampstand itself are four cups shaped like almond blossoms, with calyxes and flowers.

verses.text: and a bud under two branches of one piece with it, and a bud under two branches of one piece with it, and a bud under two branches of one piece with it, for the six branches going out of it.
verses.explainer: A calyx of one piece with it under each pair of the six branches.

verses.text: Their buds and their branches were of one piece with it. The whole thing was one beaten work of pure gold.
verses.explainer: Their calyxes and branches are of one piece with it. The whole of it is a single piece of hammered work of pure gold.

verses.text: He made its seven lamps, and its snuffers, and its snuff dishes, of pure gold.
verses.explainer: He makes its seven lamps, its tongs, and its trays of pure gold.

verses.text: He made it of a talent of pure gold, with all its vessels.
verses.explainer: He makes it and all its utensils out of a talent of pure gold. Seventy-five pounds of gold hammered into a single, intricately designed lampstand.

verses.text: He made the altar of incense of acacia wood. It was square: its length was a cubit, and its width a cubit. Its height was two cubits. Its horns were of one piece with it.
verses.explainer: He makes the altar of incense of acacia wood. A cubit its length, a cubit its breadth (it is square) and two cubits its height. Its horns are of one piece with it.

verses.text: He overlaid it with pure gold, its top, its sides around it, and its horns. He made a gold molding around it.
verses.explainer: He overlays it with pure gold -- its top, its sides all around, and its horns. He makes a molding of gold around it.

verses.text: He made two golden rings for it under its molding crown, on its two ribs, on its two sides, for places for poles with which to carry it.
verses.explainer: He makes two golden rings for it under its molding, on two opposite sides, as holders for the poles to carry it.

verses.text: He made the poles of acacia wood, and overlaid them with gold.
verses.explainer: He makes the poles of acacia wood and overlays them with gold.

verses.text: He made the holy anointing oil and the pure incense of sweet spices, after the art of the perfumer.
verses.explainer: He makes the holy anointing oil and the pure fragrant incense, blended as by the perfumer. The sacred oils and incense prepared exactly as commanded.

explainer.summary: The outer furnishings are constructed: the bronze altar of burnt offering, the bronze basin, and the courtyard with its posts, hangings, and gate screen. The chapter concludes with a detailed accounting of all the materials used -- nearly a ton of gold, over three and a half tons of silver, and over two and a half tons of bronze. The meticulous inventory demonstrates transparency and accountability in the use of donated resources.
themes: The construction of the outer court
The enormous material investment in the Tabernacle
explainer.contextInBook: Moving outward from the Most Holy Place (ch. 37) to the courtyard (ch. 38), the construction account covers every zone of the Tabernacle complex. The materials inventory serves as a public accounting -- the people can see exactly how their gifts were used.

verses.text: He made the altar of burnt offering of acacia wood. It was square. Its length was five cubits, its width was five cubits, and its height was three cubits.
verses.explainer: He makes the altar of burnt offering of acacia wood. Five cubits its length, five cubits its breadth (it is square) and three cubits its height. The central piece of the courtyard -- the place of sacrifice.

verses.text: He made its horns on its four corners. Its horns were of one piece with it, and he overlaid it with brass.
verses.explainer: He makes horns for it on its four corners, of one piece with it, and overlays it with bronze.

verses.text: He made all the vessels of the altar, the pots, the shovels, the basins, the forks, and the fire pans. He made all its vessels of brass.
verses.explainer: He makes all the utensils of the altar: the pots, shovels, basins, forks, and fire pans, all of bronze.

verses.text: He made for the altar a grating of a network of brass, under the ledge around it beneath, reaching halfway up.
verses.explainer: He makes for the altar a grating, a network of bronze, under its ledge, extending halfway down.

verses.text: He cast four rings for the four ends of brass grating, to be places for the poles.
verses.explainer: He casts four rings on the four corners of the bronze grating as holders for the poles.

verses.text: He made the poles of acacia wood, and overlaid them with brass.
verses.explainer: He makes the poles of acacia wood and overlays them with bronze.

verses.text: He put the poles into the rings on the sides of the altar, with which to carry it. He made it hollow with planks.
verses.explainer: He puts the poles through the rings on the sides of the altar to carry it. He makes the altar hollow, with boards.

verses.text: He made the basin of brass, and its base of brass, out of the mirrors of the ministering women who ministered at the door of the Tent of Meeting.
verses.explainer: He makes the basin of bronze and its stand of bronze, from the mirrors of the ministering women who minister at the entrance of the tent of meeting. The women donate their bronze mirrors -- instruments of vanity become instruments of holiness.

verses.text: He made the court: for the south side southward the hangings of the court were of fine twined linen, one hundred cubits;
verses.explainer: He makes the court. For the south side, the hangings of the court are of fine twined linen, a hundred cubits long.

verses.text: their pillars were twenty, and their sockets twenty, of brass; the hooks of the pillars and their fillets were of silver.
verses.explainer: Their twenty pillars and twenty bases are of bronze. The hooks of the pillars and their fillets are of silver.

verses.text: For the north side one hundred cubits, their pillars twenty, and their sockets twenty, of brass; the hooks of the pillars, and their fillets, of silver.
verses.explainer: For the north side, a hundred cubits. Their twenty pillars and twenty bases are of bronze. The hooks and fillets are of silver.

verses.text: For the west side were hangings of fifty cubits, their pillars ten, and their sockets ten; the hooks of the pillars, and their fillets, of silver.
verses.explainer: For the west side, fifty cubits of hangings, with ten pillars and ten bases. The hooks and fillets are of silver.

verses.text: For the east side eastward fifty cubits.
verses.explainer: For the front, on the east side, fifty cubits.

verses.text: The hangings for the one side were fifteen cubits; their pillars three, and their sockets three;
verses.explainer: The hangings for one side are fifteen cubits, with three pillars and three bases.

verses.text: and so for the other side: on this hand and that hand by the gate of the court were hangings of fifteen cubits; their pillars three, and their sockets three.
verses.explainer: And so for the other side. On both sides of the gate of the court are hangings of fifteen cubits, with three pillars and three bases.

verses.text: All the hangings around the court were of fine twined linen.
verses.explainer: All the hangings around the court are of fine twined linen.

verses.text: The sockets for the pillars were of brass. The hooks of the pillars and their fillets were of silver; and the overlaying of their capitals, of silver; and all the pillars of the court were filleted with silver.
verses.explainer: The bases for the pillars are of bronze. The hooks and fillets are of silver. The overlaying of their capitals is of silver. All the pillars of the court are filleted with silver.

verses.text: The screen for the gate of the court was the work of the embroiderer, of blue, purple, scarlet, and fine twined linen. Twenty cubits was the length, and the height in the width was five cubits, like to the hangings of the court.
verses.explainer: The screen for the gate of the court is embroidered with needlework in blue, purple, and scarlet yarns and fine twined linen. Twenty cubits long and five cubits high, corresponding to the hangings of the court.

verses.text: Their pillars were four, and their sockets four, of brass; their hooks of silver, and the overlaying of their capitals, and their fillets, of silver.
verses.explainer: Its four pillars and four bases are of bronze, its hooks of silver, and the overlaying of their capitals and fillets of silver.

verses.text: All the pins of the tabernacle, and around the court, were of brass.
verses.explainer: All the pegs for the tabernacle and for the court all around are of bronze.

verses.text: This is the amount of material used for the tabernacle, even the Tabernacle of the Testimony, as they were counted, according to the commandment of Moses, for the service of the Levites, by the hand of Ithamar, the son of Aaron the priest.
verses.explainer: These are the records of the tabernacle, the tabernacle of the testimony, as they were recorded at the commandment of Moses by the Levites under the direction of Ithamar the son of Aaron the priest. A formal inventory -- public accountability for donated resources.

culturalNotes.title: Cultural Context
culturalNotes.note: {'term': 'Records of the tabernacle', 'explanation': 'This materials inventory represents one of the earliest examples of financial accountability for donated resources. The detailed accounting -- conducted under priestly oversight -- established the principle that those who handle sacred gifts must be transparent about how they are used. This principle echoes through religious and nonprofit governance to this day.'}

verses.text: Bezalel the son of Uri, the son of Hur, of the tribe of Judah, made all that Yahweh commanded Moses.
verses.explainer: Bezalel the son of Uri, son of Hur, of the tribe of Judah, made all that the LORD commanded Moses.

verses.text: With him was Oholiab, the son of Ahisamach, of the tribe of Dan, an engraver, and a skillful workman, and an embroiderer in blue, in purple, in scarlet, and in fine linen.
verses.explainer: And with him is Oholiab the son of Ahisamach, of the tribe of Dan, an engraver, designer, and embroiderer in blue, purple, and scarlet yarns and fine twined linen.

verses.text: All the gold that was used for the work in all the work of the sanctuary, even the gold of the offering, was twenty-nine talents, and seven hundred thirty shekels, after the shekel of the sanctuary.
verses.explainer: All the gold that is used for the work, in all the construction of the sanctuary -- the gold from the offering -- is twenty-nine talents and 730 shekels, by the shekel of the sanctuary. Nearly a ton of gold.

thenVsNow.then: Twenty-nine talents of gold is approximately 2,200 pounds (one metric ton). For a community of former slaves, this is an astounding amount, partly explained by the plundering of Egypt (12:35-36). The gold that once adorned Egyptian temples now serves the God who defeated Egypt.
thenVsNow.now: The precise accounting of materials -- gold, silver, and bronze -- establishes a principle of stewardship. Sacred resources require transparent management. Every ounce of donated gold can be traced to its use in the finished Tabernacle.

verses.text: The silver of those who were counted of the congregation was one hundred talents, and one thousand seven hundred seventy-five shekels, after the shekel of the sanctuary:
verses.explainer: The silver from those of the congregation who were recorded is a hundred talents and 1,775 shekels, by the shekel of the sanctuary. Over three and a half tons of silver.

verses.text: a beka a head, that is, half a shekel, after the shekel of the sanctuary, for everyone who passed over to those who were counted, from twenty years old and upward, for six hundred three thousand five hundred fifty men.
verses.explainer: A beka a head (half a shekel by the shekel of the sanctuary) for everyone who was listed in the records, from twenty years old and upward -- for 603,550 men.

verses.text: The one hundred talents of silver were for casting the sockets of the sanctuary, and the sockets of the veil; one hundred sockets for the one hundred talents, a talent for a socket.
verses.explainer: The hundred talents of silver are for casting the bases of the sanctuary and the bases of the veil. A hundred bases for the hundred talents, a talent a base.

verses.text: Of the one thousand seven hundred seventy-five shekels he made hooks for the pillars, overlaid their capitals, and made fillets for them.
verses.explainer: And of the 1,775 shekels he makes hooks for the pillars, overlays their capitals, and makes fillets for them.

verses.text: The brass of the offering was seventy talents, and two thousand four hundred shekels.
verses.explainer: The bronze that is offered is seventy talents and 2,400 shekels. Over two and a half tons of bronze.

verses.text: With this he made the sockets to the door of the Tent of Meeting, the bronze altar, the bronze grating for it, all the vessels of the altar,
verses.explainer: With it he makes the bases for the entrance of the tent of meeting, the bronze altar and its bronze grating, and all the utensils of the altar.

verses.text: the sockets around the court, the sockets of the gate of the court, all the pins of the tabernacle, and all the pins around the court.
verses.explainer: The bases around the court, the bases of the gate of the court, all the pegs of the tabernacle, and all the pegs around the court.

explainer.summary: The priestly garments are made: the ephod, the breastpiece with twelve stones, the robe with bells and pomegranates, the tunics, turbans, and the gold plate inscribed 'Holy to the LORD.' The chapter repeatedly emphasizes: 'as the LORD had commanded Moses.' When all the work is finished, the people bring everything to Moses. He inspects it all, sees that they have done it exactly as the LORD commanded, and blesses them.
themes: The refrain 'as the LORD commanded Moses'
Beauty in every detail of sacred worship
explainer.contextInBook: The sevenfold repetition of 'as the LORD commanded Moses' in this chapter echoes the seven days of creation. Building the Tabernacle is a new creation -- God's dwelling place, made by human hands according to a divine pattern. Moses' blessing parallels God's blessing in Genesis 1.

verses.text: Of the blue, purple, and scarlet, they made finely worked garments, for ministering in the holy place, and made the holy garments for Aaron; as Yahweh commanded Moses.
verses.explainer: From the blue, purple, and scarlet yarns they make finely worked garments for ministering in the Holy Place. They make the holy garments for Aaron, as the LORD had commanded Moses.

verses.text: He made the ephod of gold, blue, purple, scarlet, and fine twined linen.
verses.explainer: He makes the ephod of gold, blue, purple, scarlet yarns, and fine twined linen.

verses.text: They beat the gold into thin plates, and cut it into wires, to work it in with the blue, the purple, the scarlet, and the fine linen, the work of the skillful workman.
verses.explainer: They hammer out gold leaf and cut it into threads to work into the blue, purple, and scarlet yarns and fine linen, in skilled design. Gold threads woven into fabric -- extraordinary craftsmanship.

verses.text: They made shoulder straps for it, joined together. At the two ends it was joined together.
verses.explainer: They make shoulder pieces for the ephod, joined to it at its two edges.

verses.text: The skillfully woven band that was on it, with which to fasten it on, was of the same piece, like its work; of gold, of blue, purple, scarlet, and fine twined linen; as Yahweh commanded Moses.
verses.explainer: The skillfully woven band on it is of one piece with it and made like it, of gold, blue, purple, and scarlet yarns and fine twined linen, as the LORD had commanded Moses.

verses.text: They worked the onyx stones, enclosed in settings of gold, engraved with the engravings of a signet, according to the names of the children of Israel.
verses.explainer: They make the onyx stones, enclosed in settings of gold filigree, and engraved like the engravings of a signet, according to the names of the sons of Israel.

verses.text: He put them on the shoulder straps of the ephod, to be stones of memorial for the children of Israel, as Yahweh commanded Moses.
verses.explainer: He sets them on the shoulder pieces of the ephod as stones of remembrance for the sons of Israel, as the LORD had commanded Moses.

verses.text: He made the breastplate, the work of a skillful workman, like the work of the ephod; of gold, of blue, purple, scarlet, and fine twined linen.
verses.explainer: He makes the breastpiece, in skilled work, like the work of the ephod, of gold, blue, purple, and scarlet yarns and fine twined linen.

verses.text: It was square. They made the breastplate double. Its length was a span, and its width a span, being double.
verses.explainer: It is square. They make the breastpiece doubled, a span its length and a span its breadth when doubled.

verses.text: They set in it four rows of stones. A row of ruby, topaz, and beryl was the first row;
verses.explainer: They set in it four rows of stones. A row of sardius, topaz, and carbuncle is the first row.

verses.text: and the second row, a turquoise, a sapphire, and an emerald;
verses.explainer: The second row: an emerald, a sapphire, and a diamond.

verses.text: and the third row, a jacinth, an agate, and an amethyst;
verses.explainer: The third row: a jacinth, an agate, and an amethyst.

verses.text: and the fourth row, a chrysolite, an onyx, and a jasper. They were enclosed in gold settings.
verses.explainer: The fourth row: a beryl, an onyx, and a jasper. They are enclosed in settings of gold filigree. Twelve stones for twelve tribes, each uniquely beautiful.

verses.text: The stones were according to the names of the children of Israel, twelve, according to their names; like the engravings of a signet, everyone according to his name, for the twelve tribes.
verses.explainer: There are twelve stones with their names according to the names of the sons of Israel. They are like signets, each engraved with its name, for the twelve tribes.

verses.text: They made on the breastplate chains like cords, of braided work of pure gold.
verses.explainer: They make on the breastpiece twisted chains like cords, of pure gold.

verses.text: They made two settings of gold, and two gold rings, and put the two rings on the two ends of the breastplate.
verses.explainer: They make two settings of gold filigree and two gold rings, and put the two rings on the two edges of the breastpiece.

verses.text: They put the two braided chains of gold in the two rings at the ends of the breastplate.
verses.explainer: They put the two cords of gold in the two rings at the edges of the breastpiece.

verses.text: The other two ends of the two braided chains they put on the two settings, and put them on the shoulder straps of the ephod, in its front.
verses.explainer: They attach the two ends of the two cords to the two settings of filigree on the shoulder pieces of the ephod, in front.

verses.text: They made two rings of gold, and put them on the two ends of the breastplate, on its edge, which was toward the side of the ephod inward.
verses.explainer: They make two rings of gold and put them at the two ends of the breastpiece, on its inside edge next to the ephod.

verses.text: They made two more rings of gold, and put them on the two shoulder straps of the ephod underneath, in its front, close by its coupling, above the skillfully woven band of the ephod.
verses.explainer: They make two rings of gold and attach them in front to the lower part of the two shoulder pieces of the ephod, at its seam above the skillfully woven band.

verses.text: They bound the breastplate by its rings to the rings of the ephod with a lace of blue, that it might be on the skillfully woven band of the ephod, and that the breastplate might not come loose from the ephod, as Yahweh commanded Moses.
verses.explainer: They bind the breastpiece by its rings to the rings of the ephod with a lace of blue, so that it should lie on the skillfully woven band, and the breastpiece should not come loose, as the LORD had commanded Moses.

verses.text: He made the robe of the ephod of woven work, all of blue.
verses.explainer: He makes the robe of the ephod woven all of blue.

verses.text: The opening of the robe in the middle of it was like the opening of a coat of mail, with a binding around its opening, that it should not be torn.
verses.explainer: The opening of the robe in it is like the opening in a garment, with a binding around the opening, so that it might not tear.

verses.text: They made on the skirts of the robe pomegranates of blue, purple, scarlet, and twined linen.
verses.explainer: On the hem of the robe they make pomegranates of blue, purple, and scarlet yarns and fine twined linen.

verses.text: They made bells of pure gold, and put the bells between the pomegranates around the skirts of the robe, between the pomegranates;
verses.explainer: They make bells of pure gold and put them between the pomegranates all around the hem of the robe, between the pomegranates.

verses.text: a bell and a pomegranate, a bell and a pomegranate, around the skirts of the robe, to minister in, as Yahweh commanded Moses.
verses.explainer: A bell and a pomegranate, a bell and a pomegranate around the hem of the robe for ministering, as the LORD had commanded Moses.

verses.text: They made the coats of fine linen of woven work for Aaron, and for his sons,
verses.explainer: They make the coats of fine linen for Aaron and his sons, woven of fine linen.

verses.text: and the turban of fine linen, and the linen headbands of fine linen, and the linen breeches of fine twined linen,
verses.explainer: And the turban of fine linen, the caps of fine linen, the linen undergarments of fine twined linen.

verses.text: and the sash of fine twined linen, and blue, and purple, and scarlet, the work of the embroiderer, as Yahweh commanded Moses.
verses.explainer: The sash of fine twined linen and of blue, purple, and scarlet yarns, embroidered with needlework, as the LORD had commanded Moses.

verses.text: They made the plate of the holy crown of pure gold, and wrote on it a writing, like the engravings of a signet: “HOLY TO YAHWEH”.
verses.explainer: They make the plate of the holy crown of pure gold and write on it an inscription, like the engraving of a signet: 'Holy to the LORD.' The crown of the high priest -- God's claim over the priesthood and the nation.

verses.text: They tied to it a lace of blue, to fasten it on the turban above, as Yahweh commanded Moses.
verses.explainer: They tie to it a cord of blue to fasten it on the turban above, as the LORD had commanded Moses.

verses.text: Thus all the work of the tabernacle of the Tent of Meeting was finished. The children of Israel did according to all that Yahweh commanded Moses; so they did.
verses.explainer: Thus all the work of the tabernacle of the tent of meeting is finished. The Israelites do according to all that the LORD has commanded Moses; so they do. The work is complete. The refrain echoes creation's completion: 'Thus the heavens and the earth were finished' (Genesis 2:1).

verses.text: They brought the tabernacle to Moses, the tent, with all its furniture, its clasps, its boards, its bars, its pillars, its sockets,
verses.explainer: They bring the tabernacle to Moses: the tent and all its utensils, its clasps, frames, bars, pillars, and bases.

verses.text: the covering of rams’ skins dyed red, the covering of sea cow hides, the veil of the screen,
verses.explainer: The covering of tanned rams' skins and the covering of goatskins, and the veil of the screen.

verses.text: the ark of the testimony with its poles, the mercy seat,
verses.explainer: The ark of the testimony with its poles and the mercy seat.

verses.text: the table, all its vessels, the show bread,
verses.explainer: The table and all its utensils, and the bread of the Presence.

verses.text: the pure lamp stand, its lamps, even the lamps to be set in order, all its vessels, the oil for the light,
verses.explainer: The pure lampstand with its lamps set on it, all its utensils, and the oil for the light.

verses.text: the golden altar, the anointing oil, the sweet incense, the screen for the door of the Tent,
verses.explainer: The golden altar, the anointing oil, the fragrant incense, and the screen for the entrance of the tent.

verses.text: the bronze altar, its grating of brass, its poles, all of its vessels, the basin and its base,
verses.explainer: The bronze altar and its grating, its poles and all its utensils, the basin and its stand.

verses.text: the hangings of the court, its pillars, its sockets, the screen for the gate of the court, its cords, its pins, all the instruments of the service of the tabernacle, for the Tent of Meeting,
verses.explainer: The hangings of the court, its pillars, its bases, the screen for the gate of the court, its cords, its pegs, and all the utensils for the service of the tabernacle, for the tent of meeting.

verses.text: the finely worked garments for ministering in the holy place, the holy garments for Aaron the priest, and the garments of his sons, to minister in the priest’s office.
verses.explainer: The finely worked garments for ministering in the Holy Place, the holy garments for Aaron the priest, and the garments of his sons for their service as priests.

verses.text: According to all that Yahweh commanded Moses, so the children of Israel did all the work.
verses.explainer: According to all that the LORD had commanded Moses, so the Israelites had done all the work.

verses.text: Moses saw all the work, and behold, they had done it as Yahweh had commanded, even so had they done it: and Moses blessed them.
verses.explainer: And Moses sees all the work, and behold, they have done it; as the LORD had commanded, so they have done it. Then Moses blesses them. Moses inspects and blesses the work, just as God inspected and blessed creation in Genesis 1. The parallel is intentional and complete.

explainer.summary: The culmination of the entire book. God tells Moses to set up the Tabernacle on the first day of the first month -- exactly one year after the Exodus. Moses assembles the Tabernacle, places the Ark behind the veil, sets up the table, lampstand, golden altar, and bronze altar, erects the courtyard, and anoints everything. When Moses finishes, the cloud covers the tent of meeting and the glory of the LORD fills the Tabernacle -- so powerfully that even Moses cannot enter. The cloud guides Israel's journeys: when it lifts, they travel; when it settles, they camp. The cloud of the LORD is on the Tabernacle by day, and fire by night, in the sight of all the house of Israel throughout all their journeys.
themes: The completion and dedication of God's dwelling
The presence so powerful even Moses cannot enter
The cloud and fire guiding all future journeys
The fulfillment of Exodus 25:8: I will dwell among them
explainer.contextInBook: The book that began with Israel enslaved in Egypt ends with God's glory dwelling among a free people. The Tabernacle is the answer to the Exodus: God freed Israel so he could live with them. The final image -- the cloud and fire over the Tabernacle for all their journeys -- is the perfect ending: God goes with his people, wherever they go.

wordStudies.translatedAs: The visible, weighty, radiant manifestation of God
wordStudies.meaning: The visible, weighty, radiant manifestation of God's presence. When the glory fills the Tabernacle, it is God himself taking up residence. The glory is not a symbol or a metaphor -- it is the real, experienced, overwhelming presence of God that fills the space so completely that even Moses cannot enter.

verses.text: Yahweh spoke to Moses, saying,
verses.explainer: The LORD speaks to Moses.

verses.text: “On the first day of the first month you shall raise up the tabernacle of the Tent of Meeting.
verses.explainer: On the first day of the first month you shall erect the tabernacle of the tent of meeting. The Tabernacle is erected on New Year's Day -- exactly one year after the Exodus. The first anniversary of liberation is marked by God moving in.

thenVsNow.then: Setting up the Tabernacle on the first day of the first month -- the anniversary month of the Exodus -- connected God's dwelling with God's deliverance. Freedom and presence belong together.
thenVsNow.now: The timing reinforces the message of the entire book: God saves people in order to live with them. Liberation is not the end goal; it is the means to an even greater end -- communion with God.

verses.text: You shall put the ark of the testimony in it, and you shall screen the ark with the veil.
verses.explainer: Put the ark of the testimony in it and screen the ark with the veil.

verses.text: You shall bring in the table, and set in order the things that are on it. You shall bring in the lamp stand, and light its lamps.
verses.explainer: Bring in the table and arrange it. Bring in the lampstand and set up its lamps.

verses.text: You shall set the golden altar for incense before the ark of the testimony, and put the screen of the door to the tabernacle.
verses.explainer: Put the golden altar for incense before the ark of the testimony. Set up the screen for the door of the tabernacle.

verses.text: “You shall set the altar of burnt offering before the door of the tabernacle of the Tent of Meeting.
verses.explainer: Set the altar of burnt offering before the door of the tabernacle of the tent of meeting.

verses.text: You shall set the basin between the Tent of Meeting and the altar, and shall put water therein.
verses.explainer: Place the basin between the tent of meeting and the altar and put water in it.

verses.text: You shall set up the court around it, and hang up the screen of the gate of the court.
verses.explainer: Set up the court all around and hang up the screen for the gate of the court.

verses.text: “You shall take the anointing oil, and anoint the tabernacle, and all that is in it, and shall make it holy, and all its furniture: and it will be holy.
verses.explainer: Take the anointing oil and anoint the tabernacle and all that is in it, and consecrate it and all its utensils. It shall become holy.

verses.text: You shall anoint the altar of burnt offering, with all its vessels, and sanctify the altar: and the altar will be most holy.
verses.explainer: Anoint the altar of burnt offering and all its utensils. Consecrate the altar, and the altar shall be most holy.

verses.text: You shall anoint the basin and its base, and sanctify it.
verses.explainer: Anoint the basin and its stand and consecrate it.

verses.text: “You shall bring Aaron and his sons to the door of the Tent of Meeting, and shall wash them with water.
verses.explainer: Bring Aaron and his sons to the entrance of the tent of meeting and wash them with water.

verses.text: You shall put on Aaron the holy garments; and you shall anoint him, and sanctify him, that he may minister to me in the priest’s office.
verses.explainer: Put the holy garments on Aaron and anoint him and consecrate him, that he may serve me as priest.

verses.text: You shall bring his sons, and put coats on them.
verses.explainer: Bring his sons and put coats on them.

verses.text: You shall anoint them, as you anointed their father, that they may minister to me in the priest’s office. Their anointing shall be to them for an everlasting priesthood throughout their generations.”
verses.explainer: Anoint them, as you anointed their father, that they may serve me as priests. Their anointing shall admit them to a perpetual priesthood throughout their generations. The Aaronic priesthood is established for all future generations.

verses.text: Moses did so. According to all that Yahweh commanded him, so he did.
verses.explainer: Moses does this; according to all that the LORD commands him, so he does. Total obedience.

verses.text: In the first month in the second year, on the first day of the month, the tabernacle was raised up.
verses.explainer: In the first month in the second year, on the first day of the month, the tabernacle is erected. One year from the Exodus.

verses.text: Moses raised up the tabernacle, and laid its sockets, and set up its boards, and put in its bars, and raised up its pillars.
verses.explainer: Moses erects the tabernacle. He lays its bases, sets up its frames, puts in its bars, and raises up its pillars.

verses.text: He spread the covering over the tent, and put the roof of the tabernacle above on it, as Yahweh commanded Moses.
verses.explainer: He spreads the tent over the tabernacle and puts the covering of the tent over it, as the LORD had commanded Moses.

verses.text: He took and put the testimony into the ark, and set the poles on the ark, and put the mercy seat above on the ark.
verses.explainer: He takes the testimony and puts it into the ark, sets the poles on the ark, and puts the mercy seat above on the ark. The most sacred act: placing God's words inside God's dwelling.

verses.text: He brought the ark into the tabernacle, and set up the veil of the screen, and screened the ark of the testimony, as Yahweh commanded Moses.
verses.explainer: He brings the ark into the tabernacle and sets up the veil of the screen and screens the ark of the testimony, as the LORD had commanded Moses.

verses.text: He put the table in the Tent of Meeting, on the side of the tabernacle northward, outside of the veil.
verses.explainer: He puts the table in the tent of meeting, on the north side of the tabernacle, outside the veil.

verses.text: He set the bread in order on it before Yahweh, as Yahweh commanded Moses.
verses.explainer: He arranges the bread on it before the LORD, as the LORD had commanded Moses.

verses.text: He put the lamp stand in the Tent of Meeting, opposite the table, on the side of the tabernacle southward.
verses.explainer: He puts the lampstand in the tent of meeting, opposite the table on the south side.

verses.text: He lit the lamps before Yahweh, as Yahweh commanded Moses.
verses.explainer: He sets up the lamps before the LORD, as the LORD had commanded Moses.

verses.text: He put the golden altar in the Tent of Meeting before the veil;
verses.explainer: He puts the golden altar in the tent of meeting before the veil.

verses.text: and he burned incense of sweet spices on it, as Yahweh commanded Moses.
verses.explainer: He burns fragrant incense on it, as the LORD had commanded Moses.

verses.text: He put up the screen of the door to the tabernacle.
verses.explainer: He puts in place the screen for the door of the tabernacle.

verses.text: He set the altar of burnt offering at the door of the tabernacle of the Tent of Meeting, and offered on it the burnt offering and the meal offering, as Yahweh commanded Moses.
verses.explainer: He sets the altar of burnt offering at the entrance of the tabernacle and offers on it the burnt offering and the grain offering, as the LORD had commanded Moses.

verses.text: He set the basin between the Tent of Meeting and the altar, and put water therein, with which to wash.
verses.explainer: He sets the basin between the tent of meeting and the altar and puts water in it for washing.

verses.text: Moses, Aaron, and his sons washed their hands and their feet there.
verses.explainer: Moses and Aaron and his sons wash their hands and their feet from it.

verses.text: When they went into the Tent of Meeting, and when they came near to the altar, they washed, as Yahweh commanded Moses.
verses.explainer: When they go into the tent of meeting and when they approach the altar, they wash, as the LORD had commanded Moses.

verses.text: He raised up the court around the tabernacle and the altar, and set up the screen of the gate of the court. So Moses finished the work.
verses.explainer: He erects the court around the tabernacle and the altar and sets up the screen of the gate of the court. So Moses finishes the work. The final construction sentence. The Tabernacle is complete.

verses.text: Then the cloud covered the Tent of Meeting, and Yahweh’s glory filled the tabernacle.
verses.explainer: Then the cloud covers the tent of meeting, and the glory of the LORD fills the tabernacle. The climax of the book. God shows up. The cloud that led them from Egypt, that descended on Sinai, now takes up permanent residence in the Tabernacle. God moves in.

verses.text: Moses wasn’t able to enter into the Tent of Meeting, because the cloud stayed on it, and Yahweh’s glory filled the tabernacle.
verses.explainer: Moses is not able to enter the tent of meeting because the cloud has settled on it and the glory of the LORD fills the tabernacle. Even Moses -- who spoke with God face to face -- cannot enter when the glory fills the Tabernacle. God's presence in its fullness exceeds the capacity of even the greatest human being.

verses.text: When the cloud was taken up from over the tabernacle, the children of Israel went onward, throughout all their journeys;
verses.explainer: Throughout all their journeys, whenever the cloud is taken up from over the tabernacle, the Israelites set out.

verses.text: but if the cloud wasn’t taken up, then they didn’t travel until the day that it was taken up.
verses.explainer: But if the cloud is not taken up, they do not set out until the day it is taken up. They move only when God moves.

verses.text: For the cloud of Yahweh was on the tabernacle by day, and there was fire in the cloud by night, in the sight of all the house of Israel, throughout all their journeys.
verses.explainer: For the cloud of the LORD is on the tabernacle by day, and fire is in it by night, in the sight of all the house of Israel throughout all their journeys. The final verse of Exodus. The book ends not with arrival but with journey -- God present with his people for every step. By day and by night, visible to all, the presence of God goes with them. The book that began in slavery ends in glory.

thenVsNow.then: The cloud and fire over the Tabernacle meant that every Israelite, from the greatest to the least, could look up at any time -- day or night -- and see visible proof that God was with them. The divine presence was not hidden, not theoretical, not a matter of faith alone. It was there.
thenVsNow.now: The final image of Exodus is the most hopeful image in the Old Testament: God does not stay on the mountain. He comes down, moves in, and travels with his people through the wilderness. The journey is not over, but the Presence is guaranteed.
